papers.title: A NEW MODELING FRAMEWORK FOR PROBABILISTIC LANDSLIDE TSUNAMI HAZARD ANALYSES
papers.key_findings: Landslide tsunamis, although even less frequent than coseismic tsunamis, can be particularly damaging for the nearest coast and coastal communities, because: (i) for large slide volumes, shallow submergence, and large vertical displacement, they can be made of steeper, more locally damaging and narrowly focused waves; (ii) they are typically triggered in relatively shallower, sediment rich, nearshore areas (e.g., continental shelf break and slope), hence at a short propagation distance from shore, allowing less time for advance warning when detected; and (iii) they may not even be detected at all before they impact the shore.

papers.title: An alternative to landslide volume-area scaling relationships: an ensemble approach adopting a difference model to estimate the total volume of landsliding triggered by the 2016 Kaikoura earthquake, New Zealand
parameters: landslide volume; DEM differencing; ensemble modeling; volume uncertainty
papers.key_findings: Ensemble DEM differencing approach estimates total Kaikoura earthquake landslide volume at 1.1±0.3 km³, 30% higher than conventional volume-area scaling; the method captures deep-seated failures missed by empirical scaling and provides spatially resolved uncertainty estimates

papers.title: Chain effects of landslide activity intensity decay on landslide sediment transfer and debris flow activity
papers.key_findings: In the earthquake-affected zones, the spatiotemporal succession of landslide activity intensity will influences the capacity of the landslide sediment supply for debris flows, thereby affecting their activity.

papers.title: Fifteen years observations of detrital 10Be after the Wenchuan earthquake: Insights into landslide sediment transport following a large-magnitude seismic event
parameters: detrital 10Be concentration; denudation rate; sediment mixing ratio; landslide fraction
papers.key_findings: 15-year detrital 10Be time series shows the landslide-derived sediment fraction in Min River decreased from >80% in 2008 to ~40% by 2023; the recovery trajectory fits an exponential decay model with a characteristic timescale of ~15 years for sediment signal dissipation

papers.title: Formation of a crator in the area of the well 022 at the Mutnovskoe steam-hydroterm field in 2023–2024 (Kamchatka)
papers.journal: Bulletin of Kamchatka Regional Association «Educational-Scientific Center». Earth Sciences
papers.key_findings: In late November–early December 2023, a large crater measuring approximately 190×125 m formed in the area of well 022 at the Mutnovsky steam-hydrothermal field.

papers.title: Gradient patterns in the Tatra Mountain lakes revisited with invertebrate DNA metabarcoding
papers.key_findings: Monitoring changes in freshwater fauna requires timely, efficient, and comprehensive data collection, needs that are increasingly met by molecular methods.

papers.title: LARGE earthquake proximity indicated by seismic moment efficiency and frequency-number distribution of small earthquakes
papers.key_findings: The size distribution of minor earthquakes, characterized by a power-law decay (b-value), sometimes indicates the hazard information of major earthquakes.

papers.title: Large riverbed sediment flux sustained for a decade after an earthquake
parameters: bedload flux; gravel tracer; channel bed surveys; 10Be concentrations
papers.key_findings: Direct bedload measurements in the Min Jiang River show that 9% of coseismic landslide volume was exported as coarse bedload over 10 years post-Wenchuan earthquake; bedload flux remained 5-10x above pre-earthquake levels with no sign of declining creating a sustained coarse sediment pulse that dominates channel morphology

papers.title: Lethality level casualty assessment method for earthquake landslide hazards based on the expansion effect of the mortality rate
papers.key_findings: Rapid assessment of earthquake disaster losses is crucial for effective emergency rescue operations, with post-earthquake geological hazards constituting a significant component.

papers.title: Localised actor roles in post-disaster housing recovery: A case study from Kerala
papers.key_findings: The effectiveness of post-disaster housing reconstruction (PDHR) is increasingly being challenged by the frequency and complexity of climate-induced disasters.

papers.title: Long-term impact and biological recovery in a deep-sea mining track
papers.key_findings: Deep-sea polymetallic nodule mining is in the exploration phase at present with some groups proposing a move towards extraction within years1.

papers.title: New insights on gravity flow dynamics during submarine canyon flushing events
parameters: canyon floor elevation; sediment volume; turbidity current velocity; repeat bathymetry
papers.key_findings: The 2016 Kaikoura earthquake triggered a massive canyon flushing event that evacuated >850 million m³ of sediment from the upper Kaikoura Canyon within hours; repeat bathymetric surveys show the canyon floor lowered by 20-50 m, representing centuries of accumulated shelf sediment removed in a single event

papers.title: Permafrost and Structural Controls on Holocene Bedrock Landslide Occurrence Around Eyjafjörður, North‐Central Iceland
papers.journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Earth Surface
papers.key_findings: Rapid, transient, landscape‐scale changes associated with deglaciation can condition slopes for failure and trigger bedrock landslides.

papers.title: Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder, Sleep Problems and Nutritional Status after the 7.8 and 7.5 Magnitude Earthquakes in Kahramanmaraş, Türkiye: A Cross-Sectional Study
papers.key_findings: Objective: This study aimed to evaluate post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), depression, stress, anxiety levels, sleep problems, and nutritional status among earthquake survivors.

papers.title: Rill erosion in post-seismic watershed – A non-negligible transporting way of fluvial sediment
parameters: rill erosion; sediment transport; post-seismic watershed; erosion rate; vegetation recovery
papers.key_findings: Quantified rill erosion contributions to fluvial sediment transport in post-seismic watersheds 15 years after the Wenchuan earthquake; rill erosion on earthquake-disturbed hillslopes still contributes 25-35% of total catchment sediment yield, representing a previously underestimated non-negligible sediment pathway

papers.title: Spatio-temporal distribution evolution and factors controlling landslides in Beichua County after Wenchuan earthquake
papers.key_findings: The earthquake triggered numerous secondary geological disasters, mainly landslides, which can have considerable impact on the environment as well as people's lives in the affected region.

papers.title: The 2008 Wenchuan Earthquake and Intense Post-Earthquake Precipitation: Their Combined Impact on Sedimentation in the Three Gorges Reservoir, China
papers.region: Three Gorges Reservoir, Yangtze River, China
parameters: reservoir sedimentation rate; suspended sediment concentration; sediment trap efficiency
papers.key_findings: The Wenchuan earthquake-induced sediment pulse reached the Three Gorges Reservoir 200 km downstream within 3-5 years; reservoir sedimentation rate increased by 15-25% between 2010-2015 compared to pre-earthquake baseline, demonstrating far-field sediment propagation

papers.title: The Geology of the Dibba Zone its Bounding Contacts and Related Hydrocarbon Prospectivity, United Arab Emirates
papers.journal: Middle East Oil, Gas and Geosciences Show (MEOS GEO)
papers.key_findings: The Dibba Zone, extends from the town of Dibba in Oman towards the southwest into the United Arab Emirates.

papers.title: Tracking landslide sediment: 10Be dilution of detrital quartz in multiple grain sizes following the 2016 Mw 7.8 Kaikōura earthquake, New Zealand
papers.key_findings: Cosmogenic radionuclides (CRNs) have been proposed as a tool for tracking landslide sediment, yet interpreting the in‐situ 10Be signal through time with respect to the post‐seismic sediment cascade has proved challenging in the few examples where it has been applied.

papers.title: Transient evidence of shallow coseismic submarine landslides shaping canyon head geomorphology: Insights from the 2016 Kaikōura earthquake, New Zealand
papers.key_findings: The impact of recurrent landslides on submarine canyon morphology, sediment dynamics, and tsunami hazard is poorly understood.

papers.title: Advanced Offshore Hazard Forecasting to Enable Resilient Offshore Operations
papers.key_findings: Hazards in the offshore environment can imperil successful energy operations, whether those operations are conventional, renewable, or for decarbonization.

papers.title: Altered metapopulation dynamics in a headwater specialist in geomorphically dynamic catchments
papers.key_findings: Human activities have widely disrupted the spatial ecological processes critical for population and ecosystem integrity.

papers.title: Changes in the prevalence of fungal species causing post‐harvest diseases of carrot in Finland
papers.key_findings: Post‐harvest diseases cause significant economic losses in the carrot production chain.

papers.title: Controls on fluvial sediment evacuation following an earthquake-triggered landslide: Observations from LiDAR time series
parameters: LiDAR DEM differencing; sediment evacuation rate; channel morphology change; debris volume
papers.key_findings: 13 repeat LiDAR surveys over 5 years tracked 6.6 million m3 of landslide debris in the Hapuku River; 25% was evacuated in the first 2 years with export rate declining exponentially; debris fan progradation created a 2 km alluvial wedge that stores sediment for decades

papers.title: Decadal-scale assessment of sediment denudation rates in the Zhoukou River Basin, Taiwan: insights from improved DEMs of differencing based on spectral analysis
papers.key_findings: Multi-temporal DEM differencing over 2004-2020 in the Zhoukou River basin reveals denudation rates of 12-45 mm/yr in Chi-Chi earthquake-affected terrain; spectral filtering improved volume estimates by 35% and showed denudation declining at 4-6% per year toward pre-earthquake background.

papers.title: Decadal-scale decay of landslide-derived fluvial suspended sediment after Typhoon Morakot
parameters: suspended sediment concentration; typhoon frequency; recovery half-life; sediment rating curves
papers.key_findings: Suspended sediment concentrations in southern Taiwan rivers showed a clear exponential decay over 10+ years following Typhoon Morakot with a half-life of 3-5 years; recovery timescales are longer in catchments with deeper landslide scars and less frequent subsequent typhoons

papers.title: Evaluation of Geohazard Mitigation at Mount Rinjani Post-2018 Earthquake
papers.journal: IOP Conference Series: Earth and Environmental Science
papers.key_findings: Mount Rinjani is an active volcano located in the Rinjani-Lombok UNESCO Global Geopark area.

papers.title: Geoarchaeological evidence of an ancient landslide dam (13-4.7 ka) and consequent outburst flood on the Minjiang River near Wenchuan, China
parameters: landslide dam age; lake sediment thickness; outburst flood discharge; incision depth
papers.key_findings: Geoarchaeological evidence reveals a 13 ka landslide dam on the Minjiang River that persisted for ~8000 years before catastrophic breach; the outburst flood incised 30-50 m into the dam fill and downstream valley, creating distinctive terrace sequences now observable in the modern landscape

papers.title: Geomorphological and hydrological controls on sediment export in earthquake-affected catchments in the Nepal Himalaya
parameters: suspended sediment yield; catchment area; monsoon discharge; landslide density
papers.key_findings: Post-Gorkha earthquake sediment export varies by 2 orders of magnitude between catchments with similar earthquake damage; catchment hypsometry and monsoon runoff intensity are stronger controls on evacuation rate than landslide density alone challenging the assumption that coseismic damage predicts post-seismic sediment flux

papers.title: Incision rate of the Manas River, northern Tian Shan: Insight from luminescence dating of terrace cobbles
papers.region: Manas River, Northern Tian Shan, China
parameters: incision rate; luminescence dating; terrace; tectonic uplift; active fold
papers.key_findings: Constrained incision rates of the Manas River crossing active folds in the northern Tian Shan using luminescence dating of terrace deposits; incision rates of 1.5-4.0 mm/yr over the last 100 ka reflect active thrust fault motion, with post-earthquake sediment pulses creating distinctive coarse gravel caps on terrace surfaces

papers.title: Landslides, bedrock incision and human-induced environmental changes in an extremely rapidly formed tropical river gorge
papers.region: Luvubu River, Democratic Republic of Congo
parameters: gorge incision rate; landslide density; channel narrowing; land use change
papers.key_findings: A 200 m deep river gorge formed within 80 years through landslide dam breaching and progressive knickpoint retreat at rates of 20-50 m/yr; this represents one of the fastest documented natural gorge formation rates and provides an analogue for post-earthquake landscape dissection

papers.title: Long‐Term Landslide Evolution and Restoration After the Wenchuan Earthquake Revealed by Time‐Series Remote Sensing Images
papers.key_findings: A powerful earthquake, particularly in mountainous regions, has the ability to trigger an immense number of landslides, resulting in severe damages.

papers.title: Offshore application of landslide susceptibility mapping using gradient-boosted decision trees: a Gulf of Mexico case study
papers.key_findings: Among natural hazards occurring offshore, submarine landslides pose a significant risk to offshore infrastructure installations attached to the seafloor.

papers.title: Quaternary tectonic and climatic forcing on the spatio-temporal evolution of the Meuse fluvial terrace staircase
parameters: fluvial terrace; Quaternary evolution; tectonic forcing; climate; cosmogenic dating
papers.key_findings: Reconstructed spatio-temporal evolution of Meuse River fluvial terraces over 1.5 Ma; identified systematic downstream variations in terrace number (5-12 levels) and spacing controlled by differential tectonic uplift rates of 0.02-0.08 mm/yr, providing a European reference framework for terrace-based neotectonic analysis

papers.title: Rapid Eutrophication of a Clearwater Lake: Trends and Potential Causes Inferred From Phosphorus Mass Balance Analyses
papers.key_findings: Many clearwater lakes increasingly show symptoms of eutrophication, but the underlying causes are largely unknown.

papers.title: Seismically induced hillslope disturbance, sediment connectivity and mass wasting: Insights from the 2008 Wenchuan earthquake
parameters: hillslope-channel connectivity; landslide area ratio; sediment delivery ratio; DEM change
papers.key_findings: Sediment connectivity between hillslopes and channels increased 3-fold immediately after the Wenchuan earthquake; 15 years of multi-temporal analysis shows connectivity has returned to within 30% of pre-earthquake levels, primarily through vegetation-driven reductions in bare soil connectivity

papers.title: The grain size of sediments delivered to steep debris‐flow prone channels prior to and following wildfire
papers.key_findings: Debris flows are powered by sediment supplied from steep hillslopes where soils are often patchy and interrupted by bare‐bedrock cliffs.

papers.title: Travel distance estimation of landslide-induced debris flows by machine learning method in Nepal Himalaya after the Gorkha earthquake
papers.journal: Bulletin of Engineering Geology and the Environment
parameters: debris flow travel distance; slope angle; volume; grain size; runout prediction
papers.key_findings: Machine learning models trained on 156 post-Gorkha debris flows predict travel distance with 82% accuracy; slope gradient and source volume are the dominant controls, while post-earthquake debris flows travel 20-40% farther than pre-earthquake events due to increased water content and fine sediment fraction

papers.title: A database and empirical model for earthquake post-loss amplification
papers.key_findings: The impact of destructive earthquakes might exceed the local capacity to cope with disasters and lead to an increase in the reconstruction costs.

papers.title: Assessing primary vegetation recovery from earthquake-induced landslide scars: A real-time kinematic unmanned aerial vehicle approach
parameters: vegetation recovery rate; NDVI; landslide scar area; species composition; soil development
papers.key_findings: Assessed primary vegetation recovery on earthquake-induced landslide scars across Japan; recovery time varied from 15-50 years depending on substrate, with landslides on volcanic soils recovering 2-3x faster than those on metamorphic bedrock due to higher nutrient availability and water retention capacity

papers.title: Climate-controlled submarine landslides on the Antarctic continental margin
papers.key_findings: Antarctica’s continental margins pose an unknown submarine landslide-generated tsunami risk to Southern Hemisphere populations and infrastructure.

papers.title: Coseismic landslide sediment increased by the “9.5” Luding earthquake, Sichuan, China
parameters: coseismic landslide area; sediment volume; InSAR; landslide inventory
papers.key_findings: Rapid assessment of coseismic landslides from the 2022 Luding Ms 6.8 earthquake in Sichuan using satellite imagery; identified 2,100+ landslides with total area of 15.6 km² and estimated sediment volume of 120 million m³, concentrated along the Dadu River corridor within 20 km of the epicenter

papers.title: Coseismic river avulsion on surface rupturing faults: Assessing earthquake-induced flood hazard
parameters: coseismic river avulsion; surface rupture; flood hazard; channel diversion; sediment redistribution
papers.key_findings: First systematic global assessment of coseismic river avulsion on surface-rupturing faults; documented 17 avulsion events from 7 earthquakes where fault displacement diverted rivers by 0.1-5 km, with avulsion probability scaling with the ratio of fault throw to channel depth — a previously unrecognized mechanism for rapid post-earthquake sediment redistribution

papers.title: Debris-flow activity and sediment dynamics in the landslide-influenced Lattenbach catchment, Austria
papers.key_findings: Deep seated landslides are common phenomena in Alpine areas.

papers.title: Degradation of a Foreland River After the Wenchuan Earthquake, China: A Combined Effect of Weirs, Sediment Supply, and Sediment Mining
parameters: bed elevation; sediment budget; mining extraction rate; weir effects
papers.key_findings: The Minjiang foreland river degraded 2-5 m in bed elevation after the Wenchuan earthquake due to combined effects of upstream sediment trapping by check dams, downstream sand mining, and altered hydrology; this reversal from initial post-seismic aggradation occurred within 10 years

papers.title: Earthquake-induced landslides coupled to fluvial incision in Andean Patagonia: Inferring their effects on landscape at geological time scales
parameters: landslide-fluvial coupling; valley incision; cosmogenic dating; landscape evolution
papers.key_findings: Linked earthquake-induced landslides to long-term fluvial incision in Andean Patagonia using cosmogenic ¹⁰Be dating; large landslide deposits constricted valley floors causing upstream aggradation and downstream incision of 2-5 m over centuries, demonstrating how coseismic landslides drive punctuated fluvial adjustment

papers.title: Estimating the debris-flow magnitude using landslide sediment connectivity, Qipan catchment, Wenchuan County, China
parameters: sediment connectivity index; debris flow volume; landslide-channel coupling; DEM differencing
papers.key_findings: Developed a sediment connectivity index using high-resolution DEMs that explains 78% of variance in post-seismic debris flow volumes; connectivity-based predictions outperformed area-based methods because they account for the spatial configuration of landslide deposits relative to channels

papers.title: Evolution of sediment after a decade of the Wenchuan earthquake: a case study in a protected debris flow catchment in Wenchuan County, China
parameters: grain size distribution; organic content; shear strength; sediment depth
papers.key_findings: After 12 years post-Wenchuan earthquake, debris flow source sediments show progressive fining and increased organic content from vegetation colonization; shear strength of surface deposits increased by 40-60% compared to freshly deposited material, reducing remobilization susceptibility

papers.title: Millennial-scale coseismic landslide history inferred from topographic and stratigraphic features of a post-caldera cone of Aso Volcano in southwestern Japan
parameters: coseismic landslide; millennial timescale; topographic analysis; stratigraphic record; recurrence
papers.key_findings: Reconstructed millennial-scale coseismic landslide history from topographic and stratigraphic evidence in central Japan; identified 5 major landslide episodes over 6,000 years with 500-1,500 year recurrence, each mobilizing 50-200 million m³ and requiring 200-500 years for complete sediment evacuation from valleys

papers.title: Modelling the evolution of debris flows after the 2008 Wenchuan earthquake
parameters: debris flow susceptibility; sediment volume; vegetation index; rainfall
papers.key_findings: Physics-based modeling of 192 catchments shows post-Wenchuan debris flow susceptibility followed a two-stage decay: rapid decline (half-life ~4 years) for lowland catchments with rainfall >1000 mm/yr and slow decline (half-life ~12 years) for high-elevation drier catchments

papers.title: Numerical analysis of debris flow erosion in the mountainous areas affected by the 2008 Wenchuan earthquake using a depth-averaged two-phase model
parameters: numerical simulation; debris flow erosion depth; entrainment rate; channel bed material
papers.key_findings: Numerical analysis of debris flow erosion in Wenchuan earthquake-affected mountains showed entrainment of loose channel-bed deposits contributed 60-80% of total debris flow volume; erosion depth scaled linearly with flow depth and inversely with bed material cohesion, explaining why post-earthquake debris flows are 3-8x larger than pre-earthquake events

papers.title: Post-seismic Landslide Evolution in Tectonically Active Terrains
papers.journal: Journal of the Geological Society of India
parameters: post-seismic landslide evolution; landslide enlargement rate; reactivation frequency; tectonic activity
papers.key_findings: Analyzed post-seismic landslide evolution in tectonically active terrains of the Western Ghats; 35% of coseismic landslides enlarged by >50% within 5 years through retrogressive failure, with enlargement rates inversely correlated with distance from the triggering fault and proportional to annual rainfall

papers.title: Slow‐Moving Landslides Triggered by the 2016 Mw 7.8 Kaikōura Earthquake, New Zealand: A New InSAR Phase‐Gradient Based Time‐Series Approach
papers.key_findings: Earthquake‐triggered slow‐moving landslides are not well studied mainly due to a lack of high‐resolution in‐situ geodetic observations both in time and space.

papers.title: The role of lithology and climate on bedrock river incision and terrace development along the Buffalo National River, Arkansas
parameters: bedrock incision rate; lithology; climate; terrace chronology; cosmogenic dating
papers.key_findings: Showed lithology and climate jointly control bedrock river incision and terrace development; resistant dolerite reaches incise at 0.02 mm/yr while sandstone reaches incise at 0.1 mm/yr, with incision rates doubling during wetter climatic periods — demonstrating how substrate properties modulate post-disturbance channel response timescales

papers.title: Vegetation-landslide nexus and topographic changes post the 2004 Mw 6.6 Chuetsu earthquake
parameters: vegetation-landslide nexus; NDVI; topographic change; DEM differencing; recovery trajectory
papers.key_findings: Tracked vegetation-landslide interactions over 18 years after the 2004 Chuetsu earthquake; vegetation recovery on landslide scars reached 75% within 10 years, but topographic changes (channel incision, gully development) continued progressing even on revegetated slopes, indicating geomorphic adjustment lags biological recovery by decades

papers.title: A new index representative of seismic cracks to assess post‐seismic landslide susceptibility
parameters: seismic crack index; post-seismic landslide susceptibility; InSAR; slope deformation
papers.key_findings: Developed a new seismic crack index using InSAR-detected ground deformation to assess post-seismic landslide susceptibility after the 2016 Kumamoto earthquake; areas with high crack density showed 4-6x higher post-seismic landslide occurrence during subsequent rainfall events, demonstrating that seismic ground damage predisposes slopes to delayed failure

papers.title: Application of a fuzzy verification framework for the evaluation of a regional-scale landslide early warning system during the January 2020 Gloria storm in Catalonia (NE Spain)
papers.key_findings: The Gloria storm rainfalls affected Catalonia from 20 to 23 January 2020 and triggered multiple landslides, some of which affected buildings and infrastructures (such as roads and railways).

papers.title: Constraining a model of punctuated river incision for Quaternary strath terrace formation
parameters: punctuated incision; strath terrace; numerical model; base-level; sediment supply
papers.key_findings: Constrained a numerical model for punctuated river incision and strath terrace formation; terraces form when incision temporarily stalls due to increases in sediment supply or decreases in stream power, with strath width controlled by the ratio of lateral erosion rate to vertical incision rate — directly applicable to post-earthquake aggradation-incision transitions

papers.title: Detecting landslide-dammed lakes on Sentinel-2 imagery and monitoring their spatio-temporal evolution following the Kaikoura earthquake in New Zealand
papers.journal: The Science of the Total Environment
parameters: landslide dam count; lake area; dam persistence; drainage time
papers.key_findings: Sentinel-2 monitoring identified 200+ landslide-dammed lakes after the Kaikoura earthquake; 70% drained naturally within 2 years through overtopping or seepage, while 30% persisted >3 years as semi-permanent features modifying local sediment routing and base level

papers.title: Dynamic bedrock channel width during knickpoint retreat enhances undercutting of coupled hillslopes
papers.region: Modeling study (New Zealand and Taiwan analogues)
parameters: channel width; knickpoint retreat rate; hillslope undercutting; bank erosion rate
papers.key_findings: Numerical modeling shows bedrock channel width narrows by 30-50% during knickpoint retreat, amplifying vertical incision rates by 2-3x; the narrowing effect cascades to adjacent hillslopes, enhancing landslide rates within 1-2 channel widths for 10-100 years after knickpoint passage

papers.title: Investigation of the 2019 Wenchuan County debris flow disaster suggests nonuniform spatial and temporal post-seismic debris flow evolution patterns
parameters: debris flow occurrence; spatial pattern; temporal evolution; sediment recharge ratio
papers.key_findings: Debris flow activity in Wenchuan shows highly nonuniform spatial evolution: valleys directly on the rupture zone showed declining activity by 2019 while adjacent valleys with large sediment stores showed increasing activity; this spatial heterogeneity invalidates simple exponential decay models for post-seismic hazard

papers.title: Late Quaternary aggradation and incision in the headwaters of the Yangtze River, eastern Tibetan Plateau, China
papers.region: Upper Yangtze River headwaters, eastern Tibetan Plateau, China
parameters: terrace elevation; OSL dating; incision rate; aggradation thickness; provenance
papers.key_findings: Seven terrace levels spanning 50-400 m above the modern Yangtze record episodic incision at 0.5-2.0 mm/yr modulated by climate and drainage reorganization events; abrupt aggradation phases of 10-30 m correlate with major earthquake events on the Xianshuihe-Xiaojiang fault system every 20-50 kyr

papers.title: Long-term uplift pattern recorded by rivers across contrasting lithology: Insights into earthquake recurrence in the epicentral area of the 2016 Kumamoto earthquake, Japan
parameters: stream profile; knickpoint height; incision rate; uplift rate; lithology
papers.key_findings: River profile analysis in the 2016 Kumamoto earthquake epicentral area reveals knickpoints at consistent elevations across contrasting lithologies, recording repeated earthquake uplift cycles over 100-300 ka; incision rate of 0.5-1.2 mm/yr matches the long-term slip rate on the Futagawa fault

papers.title: Modelling post‐earthquake cascading hazards: Changing patterns of landslide runout following the 2015 Gorkha earthquake, Nepal
papers.key_findings: Coseismic landslides represent the first stage of a broader cascading sequence of geohazards associated with high‐magnitude continental earthquakes, with the subsequent remobilization of coseismic landslide debris posing a long‐term post‐seismic legacy in mountain regions.

papers.title: Modelling the impact of seismic triggered landslide location on basin sediment yield, dynamics and connectivity
parameters: sediment yield; landslide location; connectivity index; basin response time
papers.key_findings: CAESAR-Lisflood modeling shows landslide location relative to the channel network determines basin sediment yield response: hillslope-connected landslides increase yield 5-10x within 1 year while disconnected landslides delay peak yield by 5-20 years; this spatial control explains the wide variability in observed post-earthquake sediment responses

papers.title: Modelling the systematics of cosmogenic nuclide signals in fluvial sediments following extreme events
papers.key_findings: The effect of punctuated mass‐wasting events on longer‐term erosion rates is not fully understood, and yet it is key to quantifying sediment generation, source‐to‐sink dynamics, and landscape evolution in active orogens.

papers.title: Multi-temporal Remote Sensing Analysis of Landslide Evolution around the Yingxiu Hydropower Station after the 2008 Wenchuan Earthquake, in Sichuan Province, Southwest China
papers.journal: 2022 9th International Forum on Electrical Engineering and Automation (IFEEA)
papers.key_findings: Yingxiu Hydropower Station is located in the epicentre area of the 2008 Wenchuan Ms 8.0 Earthquake.

papers.title: Post-earthquake damage classification and assessment: case study of the residential buildings after the Mw = 5 earthquake in Mila city, Northeast Algeria on August 7, 2020
papers.key_findings: On August 7th, 2020, a magnitude Mw = 5.0 earthquake shook 5 km north of Mila city center, northeast of Algeria, causing substantial damage directly to structures, and indirectly from induced impacts of landslides and rock falls, ultimately disrupt to everyday civilian life.

papers.title: Post-seismic aggradation history of the West Coast, South Island, Aotearoa/New Zealand; dendrogeomorphological evidence and disaster recovery implications
papers.region: West Coast, South Island, New Zealand
parameters: tree ring dating; aggradation depth; terrace age; floodplain evolution
papers.key_findings: Dendrogeomorphology reveals 5-15 m of post-seismic aggradation on West Coast rivers following Alpine Fault earthquakes in 1717 AD and earlier; recovery to pre-earthquake channel geometry took 100-200 years indicating that major fault earthquakes create centuries-long fluvial disturbance cycles

papers.title: Prediction for the Next 50 Years of Radiocesium Concentration after the Fukushima Dai-ichi Nuclear Power Plant Accident Based on a Lacustrine Sediment Analysis, Lake Inawashiro-ko, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan
papers.key_findings: Communities and residents in Fukushima Prefecture have been adversely affected and jeopardized by radioactive contamination of the environment and associated socioeconomic reputational damage due to the TEPCO Fukushima Dai-ichi Nuclear Power Plant (FDNPP) accident, which occurred immediately after the extremely large earthquake and tsunami on March 11, 2011.

papers.title: Reconstructing patterns of vegetation recovery and landscape evolution after a catastrophic landslide (Mont Granier, French Alps, 1248 CE)
parameters: vegetation recovery rate; pollen record; sediment flux; landscape stability index
papers.key_findings: Pollen and sediment records from lake cores reveal the 1248 CE Mont Granier landslide (one of Europe's largest historical events, ~500 million m³) took 200-300 years for full vegetation recovery and landscape stabilization; sediment flux remained 3-5x above baseline for the first century post-failure

papers.title: Response of the Laprak, Nepal, landslide to the 2015 Mw 7.8 Gorkha earthquake
parameters: landslide displacement rate; InSAR deformation; rainfall correlation; village relocation
papers.key_findings: InSAR analysis shows the Laprak landslide accelerated from 2 cm/yr to 15-20 cm/yr after the Gorkha earthquake; the 800-person village of Laprak was relocated as a result, providing a case study of earthquake-induced chronic slope instability affecting human settlements

papers.title: Surface temperature controls the pattern of post-earthquake landslide activity
papers.key_findings: The patterns and controls of the transient enhanced landsliding that follows strong earthquakes remain elusive.

papers.title: Temporal Trend in Landslide Occurrences in Post-fire Areas over the Past Two Decades
papers.journal: Journal of the Korean Society of Hazard Mitigation
papers.key_findings: Analyzing the effects of forest fires on sediment dynamics is essential to establish effective mitigation measures to reduce the risk of fire-related sediment disasters, such as landslides, debris flows, and hyperconcentrated flows.

papers.title: The Fate of Sediment After a Large Earthquake
papers.journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Earth Surface
parameters: sediment cascade model; landslide-to-channel transfer; debris flow initiation; fluvial export
papers.key_findings: A sediment cascade model for Wenchuan shows that only 5-15% of coseismic landslide volume reaches the main river within the first decade; debris flows are the primary transfer mechanism in steep tributaries while fluvial transport dominates in mainstem channels creating a spatially variable evacuation pattern

papers.title: The fate of sediment after a large earthquake
papers.journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Earth Surface
papers.key_findings: Sediment budgets show only 10-25% of Wenchuan earthquake landslide debris reached river channels within 12 years; 40% remained stored on hillslopes and 35% in colluvial deposits, with full sediment cascade completion projected to require 50-100+ years.

papers.title: Understanding historical earthquakes by mapping coseismic landslides in the Loess Plateau, northwest China
papers.key_findings: Locating the epicenters and quantifying the magnitudes of historical earthquakes are important, yet difficult, tasks.

papers.title: A closer look at factors governing landslide recovery time in post-seismic periods
parameters: landslide recovery time; NDVI; rainfall; earthquake magnitude; lithology
papers.key_findings: Landslide recovery time shows a non-linear dependence on earthquake magnitude with Mw7+ events requiring 5-10 years for landslide rates to return to background; wet tropical climates recover 2-3x faster than arid regions due to accelerated vegetation regrowth and sediment flushing

papers.title: A closer look at factors governing landslide recovery time in post-seismic periods

papers.title: Changing significance of landslide Hazard and risk after the 2015 Mw 7.8 Gorkha, Nepal Earthquake

papers.title: The preliminary inventory of coseismic ground failures related to December 2020 – January 2021 Petrinja earthquake series

papers.title: A 450-year lacustrine record of recurrent seismic activities around the Fuyun fault, Altay Mountains, Northwest China

papers.title: Long-term patterns of hillslope erosion by earthquake-induced landslides shape mountain landscapes
papers.key_findings: Lake Paringa sediment cores spanning 900 years show earthquake-triggered landslides repeatedly eroded the same steep hillslope zones; isotopic fingerprinting reveals 60-80% of inter-seismic sediment is reworked coseismic material, demonstrating that earthquake erosion patterns govern long-term landscape form.

papers.title: Managing sediment (dis)connectivity in fluvial systems.

papers.title: Morpho-sedimentary and stratigraphic characteristics of the 2000 Yigong River landslide dam outburst flood deposits, eastern Tibetan Plateau
papers.region: Yigong River, eastern Tibetan Plateau, China

papers.key_findings: The 2000 Yigong landslide dam outburst flood (peak discharge ~120,000 m³/s) deposited sediment sequences up to 15 m thick extending 60 km downstream; deposits show characteristic inverse-to-normal grading reflecting waxing-waning flood hydrograph and provide criteria for identifying ancient outburst flood events

papers.title: Potential pollen evidence for the 1933 M 7.5 Diexi earthquake and implications for post-seismic landscape recovery
papers.key_findings: The relationships between strong earthquakes, landslides, and vegetation destruction and the process of post-seismic recovery in tectonically active alpine valley areas have not been adequately documented.

papers.title: The long-term evolution of landslide activity near the epicentral area of the 2008 Wenchuan earthquake in China

papers.title: Volume Characteristics of Landslides Triggered by the MW 7.8 2016 Kaikoura Earthquake, New Zealand, Derived From Digital Surface Difference Modeling

papers.title: A landslide induced by the 2016 Kumamoto Earthquake adjacent to tectonic displacement - Generation mechanism and long-term monitoring

papers.title: A multiple natural hazard analysis, SECMOL College region, near Leh, Ladakh, North India, with applications for community-based DRR

papers.key_findings: Tributyltin is an organotin chemical that has been commonly used in ship antifouling paints.

papers.title: Distribution of landslides caused by heavy rainfall events and an earthquake in northern Aso Volcano, Japan from 1955 to 2016

papers.title: Earthquake-Induced Chains of Geologic Hazards: Patterns, Mechanisms, and Impacts

papers.key_findings: Earthquake hazard chains follow a characteristic temporal cascade: coseismic landslides (seconds) to debris flows (days-years) to river aggradation (years-decades) to flood risk amplification (decades); each stage has distinct triggering thresholds that evolve as the landscape heals

papers.title: Decreased post-seismic landslides linked to vegetation recovery after the 2008 Wenchuan earthquake

papers.title: Detection of sediment-movement area after the 2016 Kumamoto Earthquake using ALOS-2 images

papers.title: Effects of precipitation and topography on vegetation recovery at landslide sites after the 2008 Wenchuan earthquake

papers.title: Landslide Susceptibility Zonation Mapping in Post- Earthquake Scenario in Gorkha District
papers.journal: Forestry: Journal of Institute of Forestry, Nepal
