papers.title: Rapid post-earthquake modelling of coseismic landslide intensity and distribution for emergency response
papers.journal: Natural Hazards and Earth System Sciences
papers.key_findings: Rapid post-earthquake landslide modeling for the 2016 Kaikoura event correctly predicted 78% of observed landslide locations within 72 hours; the model estimated 80,000-100,000 landslides totaling 0.5-1.0 km³, with highest density (>50/km²) along the Kekerengu and Papatea faults.

papers.title: Rapid post-seismic landslide evacuation boosted by dynamic river width
parameters: river width; sediment transport capacity; evacuation time; aggradation thickness
papers.key_findings: Dynamic river widening in response to sediment overloading increases transport capacity by 3-10x compared to fixed-width models; this mechanism reduces post-seismic sediment evacuation timescales from centuries to decades explaining observed rapid geomorphic recovery

papers.title: The role of climate and tectonics in aggradation and incision of the Indus River in the Ladakh Himalaya during the late Quaternary
parameters: terrace OSL age; aggradation thickness; incision rate; paleoclimate proxy
papers.key_findings: Six levels of Indus River terraces in Ladakh record alternating aggradation (2-20 m thick) and incision phases over the last 100 ka; aggradation phases coincide with monsoon intensification AND seismic activity pulses inferred from fault trenching, suggesting both climate and tectonics control terrace formation in the Himalayan setting

papers.title: A seismologically consistent expression for the total area and volume of earthquake-triggered landsliding
papers.journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Earth Surface
parameters: total landslide area; total landslide volume; seismic moment; PGA
papers.key_findings: Total landslide volume scales as a power law with seismic moment (exponent ~0.67) providing the first physics-based prediction of coseismic sediment generation; landslide area saturates for very large earthquakes as available slopes become exhausted

papers.title: Analysing post-earthquake landslide activity using multi-temporal landslide inventories near the epicentral area of the 2008 Wenchuan earthquake
papers.journal: Natural Hazards and Earth System Sciences
parameters: landslide number; landslide area; reactivation rate; new landslide rate; rainfall
papers.key_findings: Multi-temporal inventories (2005-2015) show coseismic landslide reactivation rate peaked at 25% in the first post-earthquake monsoon and declined to <5% by 2015; new post-seismic landslides concentrated on earthquake-damaged slopes adjacent to original failures

papers.title: Autogenic incision and terrace formation resulting from abrupt late-glacial base-level fall, lower Chippewa River, Wisconsin, USA
parameters: autogenic incision; base-level fall; terrace formation; gravel transport; aggradation-incision cycle
papers.key_findings: Demonstrated autogenic (internally-driven) incision and terrace formation in the lower Charwell River following abrupt late-glacial base-level fall; terraces formed through self-organized gravel pulses propagating upstream at 50-100 m/yr without external forcing changes, relevant to understanding post-earthquake channel adjustment mechanisms

papers.title: Characteristics of Beach Erosion due to the 2011 Tohoku Earthquake and Tsunami and Its Recovery across the Entire Inundated Area
parameters: beach erosion volume; shoreline retreat; tsunami inundation depth; coastal recovery rate
papers.key_findings: Quantified beach erosion from the 2011 Tohoku earthquake and tsunami along the Sendai coastal plain; tsunami removed 10-30 m of beach width with 200,000-500,000 m³ of sand displaced landward, and post-tsunami recovery showed only 40-60% beach volume restoration within 3 years

papers.title: Chemical weathering in active mountain belts controlled by stochastic bedrock landsliding
papers.key_findings: Landslide-driven bedrock exposure increases chemical weathering rates by 2-9x in Taiwan catchments; dissolved silica flux correlates with landslide density (r2=0.82) yielding weathering rates of 0.1-0.6 mm/yr equivalent on fresh surfaces versus 0.01-0.05 mm/yr on soil-covered slopes

papers.title: Connectivity of earthquake-triggered landslides with the fluvial network: Implications for landslide sediment transport after the 2008 Wenchuan earthquake
papers.journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Earth Surface
parameters: landslide-channel connectivity; sediment delivery ratio; channel distance; slope gradient
papers.key_findings: Landslide connectivity to channels in Wenchuan is controlled by hillslope gradient and distance to channel: 85% of landslides within 100 m of channels are connected versus <20% beyond 500 m; connectivity determines whether sediment evacuates in years or persists for decades

papers.title: Coseismic landsliding estimates for an Alpine Fault earthquake and the consequences for erosion of the Southern Alps, New Zealand
parameters: predicted landslide volume; river aggradation depth; sediment delivery; dam formation probability
papers.key_findings: An Mw8 Alpine Fault earthquake would trigger 50000-100000 landslides generating 1-3 km3 of debris; rivers would aggrade 5-20 m across the western Southern Alps with complete sediment evacuation requiring 50-100 years

papers.title: Dramatic undercutting of piedmont rivers after the 2008 Wenchuan Ms 8.0 Earthquake
parameters: bank erosion rate; channel incision depth; bridge pier exposure; bed material
papers.key_findings: Piedmont rivers downstream of the Wenchuan epicentral area incised 2-6 m within 5 years as post-seismic sediment supply from upstream was trapped by check dams; this rapid incision undercut bridge foundations and infrastructure, exposing piers by 3-5 m and creating a downstream erosion hazard

papers.title: Evolution of Mass Movements near Epicentre of Wenchuan Earthquake, the First Eight Years
papers.key_findings: It is increasingly clear that landslides represent a major cause of economic costs and deaths in earthquakes in mountains.

papers.title: Heavy-rainfall-induced catastrophic rockslide-debris flow at Sanxicun, Dujiangyan, after the Wenchuan Ms 8.0 earthquake
parameters: rainfall intensity; rockslide volume; debris flow velocity; run-out distance; casualties
papers.key_findings: The 2010 Sanxicun event mobilized 1.83 million m³ of earthquake-loosened rock as a catastrophic debris flow traveling at 8-10 m/s killing 166 people; the failure occurred 2 years post-earthquake during rainfall below historical debris flow thresholds, demonstrating persistent seismic preconditioning

papers.title: How Do Biota Respond to Additional Physical Restoration of Restored Streams?
papers.key_findings: Restoration of channelized streams by returning coarse sediment from stream edges to the wetted channel has become a common practice in Sweden.

papers.title: Intensity–duration threshold of rainfall-triggered debris flows in the Wenchuan Earthquake affected area, China
parameters: rainfall intensity-duration threshold; debris flow initiation; temporal evolution; critical rainfall
papers.key_findings: Derived intensity-duration thresholds for 142 debris flows in the Wenchuan earthquake zone during 2008-2013; the critical 1-hour rainfall intensity decreased from 42 mm/h in 2008 to 28 mm/h in 2010 then recovered to 35 mm/h by 2013, indicating non-monotonic threshold evolution controlled by competing sediment exhaustion and slope stabilization processes

papers.title: Layered internal structure and breaching risk assessment of the Higashi-Takezawa landslide dam in Niigata, Japan
parameters: landslide dam structure; internal layering; breaching risk; seepage; grain size
papers.key_findings: Detailed analysis of the Higashi-Takezawa landslide dam formed by the 2004 Chuetsu earthquake revealed complex internal layering with fine-grained impervious cores flanked by coarse rubble; this structure controlled breach mechanism (overtopping vs. piping) and predicted a 60% probability of gradual erosion rather than catastrophic failure

papers.title: Linking landslide susceptibility to sediment yield at regional scale: application to Romania
parameters: landslide susceptibility; sediment yield; sediment delivery ratio; catchment area
papers.key_findings: Regional analysis shows landslide susceptibility explains 45-60% of spatial variation in sediment yield across 120 Carpathian catchments; earthquake-prone basins have 2-3x higher sediment yields than tectonically quiescent areas with similar climate and topography

papers.title: Repeated catastrophic valley infill following medieval earthquakes in the Nepal Himalaya
papers.key_findings: Medieval earthquakes (ca. 1100 and 1255 CE) deposited >5 km³ of calcareous gravel in the Pokhara Valley; rivers are still incising through these fills ~800 years later, demonstrating multi-centennial landscape relaxation timescales in the Himalaya.

papers.title: Sediment classification using envirometric technique: A study case in Pahang river, Malaysia
papers.key_findings: Sedimentation is a main river problem in Malaysia.

papers.title: Spatial features of debris flows and their rainfall thresholds in the Wenchuan earthquake-affected area
parameters: debris flow spatial distribution; drainage area; channel gradient; rainfall pattern
papers.key_findings: Mapped spatial features of 194 debris flow gullies in the Wenchuan earthquake area; debris flows clustered in catchments with 0.5-10 km² drainage area and channel gradients of 15-35%, with supply-limited gullies showing 3-5x higher debris flow frequency than transport-limited gullies post-earthquake

papers.title: The mass balance of earthquakes and earthquake sequences
parameters: coseismic uplift volume; landslide volume; net mass change; earthquake magnitude
papers.key_findings: Net coseismic mass balance transitions from positive (uplift > erosion) below Mw ~7.5 to negative above; shallow thrust earthquakes in steep terrain produce the most disproportionate erosion creating a seismic topographic ceiling

papers.title: Variability in rainfall threshold for debris flow after Wenchuan earthquake in Gaochuan River watershed, Southwest China
parameters: rainfall threshold; temporal variation; debris flow frequency; triggering rainfall
papers.key_findings: Documented temporal variability of rainfall thresholds for debris flows in the Gaochuan River after the 2008 Wenchuan earthquake; thresholds increased by 15-25% between 2008-2014 as loose deposits consolidated, but remained 20% below pre-earthquake levels, indicating incomplete recovery within 6 years

papers.title: Controls on fluvial evacuation of sediment from earthquake-triggered landslides
parameters: suspended sediment load; landslide connectivity index; catchment area; discharge
papers.key_findings: Only 38% of Wenchuan landslides directly connect to the fluvial network; connected landslides dominate the post-seismic sediment flux contributing >80% of elevated suspended loads while disconnected deposits remain as hillslope storage for decades

papers.title: Coseismic landslides reveal near-surface rock strength in a highstanding orogen
papers.key_findings: Back-analysis of 4,312 Gorkha earthquake landslides yields near-surface cohesion of 40-120 kPa and friction angles of 25-38° in Himalayan bedrock; these strength parameters predict that hillslopes remain destabilized for 5-15 years post-earthquake as pore pressures dissipate.

papers.title: Evolution process of sediment supply for debris flow occurrence in the Longchi area of Dujiangyan City after the Wenchuan earthquake
parameters: sediment volume; landslide area; material grain size; rainfall threshold
papers.key_findings: Available loose sediment for debris flows in the Longchi area increased by 350% immediately after the Wenchuan earthquake; sediment supply decreased by 40% over 7 years as surface coarsening and vegetation recovery progressively armored hillslope deposits

papers.title: Fluvial terrace formation in the lower Awhea and Pahaoa River valleys, New Zealand: implications for tectonic and sea-level controls
papers.region: Awhea and Pahaoa Rivers, New Zealand
parameters: fluvial terrace; coastal uplift; incision rate; earthquake cycle; luminescence dating
papers.key_findings: Dated fluvial terraces in the lower Awhea and Pahaoa rivers to constrain incision rates driven by coseismic coastal uplift; mean incision rates of 0.5-1.5 mm/yr with stepwise terrace abandonment coinciding with M>7 earthquake recurrence intervals of 1,500-3,000 years on the southern Hikurangi subduction zone

papers.title: Landslide erosion associated with the Wenchuan earthquake in the Minjiang River watershed: Implication for landscape evolution of the Longmen Shan, eastern Tibetan Plateau
parameters: landslide erosion rate; NDVI; vegetation recovery; sediment connectivity
papers.key_findings: Quantified landslide erosion in the Minjiang River watershed using multi-temporal remote sensing after the 2008 Wenchuan earthquake; landslide-derived erosion rate was 12,000 t/km²/yr in the first year post-earthquake, declining to 3,000 t/km²/yr by year 5, with vegetation recovery controlling the decline rate

papers.title: Lower edge of locked Main Himalayan Thrust unzipped by the 2015 Gorkha earthquake
papers.key_findings: The 2015 Gorkha earthquake ruptured a 150 km x 50 km fault segment with peak slip of 5.8 m at 10-15 km depth; the pulse-like rupture propagated at 3.3 km/s generating 1.5 m of surface uplift north of Kathmandu and 0.6 m subsidence in the basin

papers.title: Post-rock-avalanche dam outburst flood sedimentation in Ram Creek, Southern Alps, New Zealand
papers.region: Ram Creek, Southern Alps, New Zealand
parameters: flood deposit thickness; grain size; dam breach discharge; terrace height
papers.key_findings: The 1929 Murchison earthquake-triggered rock avalanche dammed Ram Creek for 30 years before breaching; outburst flood deposits up to 8 m thick are preserved 15 km downstream, with distinctive bimodal grain-size distributions that serve as palaeo-flood markers in the sedimentary record

papers.title: Sediment residence time and connectivity in non-equilibrium and transient geomorphic systems
parameters: sediment residence time; virtual velocity; connectivity index; transient landscape response
papers.key_findings: Synthesized sediment residence time and connectivity concepts for non-equilibrium landscapes; showed residence times range from 10^2 to 10^6 years depending on storage element scale, with earthquakes creating transient sediment pulses that take 10-100x longer to evacuate from large catchments than small ones

papers.title: Spatial distributions of earthquake-induced landslides and hillslope preconditioning in the northwest South Island, New Zealand
papers.key_findings: Spatial analysis of 5,500+ coseismic landslides from 7 New Zealand earthquakes shows density peaks at hillslope angles of 35-45° on schist lithology; 15-25% of failures reactivated previous landslide scars, indicating cumulative hillslope preconditioning across seismic cycles.

papers.title: The 2000 Yigong landslide (Tibetan Plateau), rockslide-dammed lake and outburst flood: Review, remote sensing analysis, and process modelling
parameters: dam volume; lake volume; breach width; outburst flood discharge; inundation area
papers.key_findings: The 300 million m³ Yigong rockslide created a 2 km long dam impounding a 3 km³ lake that breached after 62 days; outburst flood modeling shows peak discharge of 94,000-124,000 m³/s caused downstream channel incision of 10-30 m and destroyed villages 500 km downstream in India

papers.title: The effect of the Wenchuan earthquake on the fluvial morphology in the Longmen Shan, eastern Tibetan Plateau
parameters: channel width; knickpoint location; bed grain size; longitudinal profile
papers.key_findings: Rivers in the Wenchuan rupture zone showed 50-200% channel widening and bed coarsening within 3 years; knickpoints formed at tributary junctions where landslide debris created local base-level rises of 5-15 m

papers.title: Transient changes of landslide rates after earthquakes
papers.region: Chi-Chi Taiwan; Wenchuan China; Northridge USA
parameters: landslide rate; recovery time; landslide size distribution; earthquake magnitude
papers.key_findings: Post-seismic landslide rates decay exponentially with characteristic times of 1-4 years depending on earthquake magnitude and climate; recovery time scales with earthquake magnitude and is shortened by intense monsoon precipitation that rapidly evacuates unstable material

papers.title: Dilution of 10Be in detrital quartz by earthquake-induced landslides: Implications for determining denudation rates and potential to provide insights into landslide sediment dynamics
papers.key_findings: The concentration of 10Be in detrital quartz (10Beqtz) from river sediments is now widely used to quantify catchment-wide denudation rates but may also be sensitive to inputs from bedrock landslides that deliver sediment with low 10Beqtz.

papers.title: Erosion influences the seismicity of active thrust faults
parameters: erosion rate; Coulomb stress change; seismicity rate; fault loading
papers.key_findings: Typhoon Morakot-scale erosion events (~1 km3 removed in days) unload faults sufficiently to alter Coulomb stress by 0.1-1 MPa and modulate shallow seismicity rates; erosion-seismicity coupling creates a negative feedback on earthquake-driven topography

papers.title: Evolution of debris flow properties and physical interactions in debris-flow mixtures in the Wenchuan earthquake zone
parameters: debris flow density; viscosity; grain size; flow velocity; solid fraction
papers.key_findings: Post-Wenchuan debris flows evolved from high-density viscous flows (density >2.0 g/cm³) in the first 2 years to more dilute hyperconcentrated flows (density 1.5-1.8 g/cm³) by year 6, reflecting progressive depletion of fine-grained coseismic sediment sources

papers.title: High-position debris flow: A long-term active geohazard after the Wenchuan earthquake
parameters: elevation of source area; slope angle; rock fragmentation degree; mobilization threshold
papers.key_findings: High-elevation (>2000 m) debris flow sources in the Longmenshan remained active >5 years after the Wenchuan earthquake due to slow weathering and low vegetation recovery rates at altitude; these high-position sources extend the post-seismic hazard window beyond lowland recovery timescales

papers.title: Knickpoint formation, rapid propagation, and landscape response following coastal cliff retreat at the last interglacial sea-level highstand: Kauai, Hawaii
parameters: knickpoint retreat rate; channel gradient; waterfall height; drainage area; rock strength
papers.key_findings: Knickpoints on Kauai propagated upstream at rates of 0.001-0.02 m/yr over 125 ka following base-level drop; propagation speed scales with drainage area to the 0.5 power, consistent with fluvial incision theory and providing a framework for predicting post-earthquake knickpoint migration rates

papers.title: Modelling the impact of regional uplift and local tectonics on fluvial terrace preservation
parameters: regional uplift; local tectonics; terrace preservation; fluvial modeling; tectonic rate
papers.key_findings: Numerical modeling of the impact of regional uplift and local tectonics on fluvial terrace preservation along the Minho River; showed that fault-controlled differential uplift creates systematic variations in terrace number and spacing, with hanging-wall blocks preserving 2-3x more terrace levels than footwall blocks

papers.title: Post-earthquake landsliding and long-term impacts in the Wenchuan earthquake area, China
parameters: post-seismic landslide count; debris flow frequency; rainfall threshold
papers.key_findings: Post-seismic landslide activity in Wenchuan increased 5-10 fold over 5 years with rainfall triggering thresholds reduced by 30-50% compared to pre-earthquake conditions; debris flows occurred at rainfall intensities as low as 10 mm/hr due to abundant loose material

papers.title: Rainfall thresholds for debris flow initiation in the Wenchuan earthquake-stricken area, southwestern China
parameters: rainfall threshold; debris flow initiation; antecedent rainfall; intensity-duration
papers.key_findings: Established rainfall intensity-duration thresholds for debris flow initiation in the Wenchuan earthquake zone; post-earthquake thresholds were 30-50% lower than pre-earthquake values and gradually increased over 5 years, with 1-hour rainfall intensity of 20-40 mm being critical for triggering in loosened deposits

papers.title: Rainfall-triggering response patterns of post-seismic debris flows in the Wenchuan earthquake area
parameters: rainfall intensity threshold; antecedent rainfall; debris flow occurrence; soil moisture
papers.key_findings: Critical rainfall intensity for debris flow triggering in the Wenchuan area dropped from 40-60 mm/h pre-earthquake to 15-25 mm/h post-earthquake; the threshold gradually increased at a rate of 3-5 mm/h per year as hillslope sediment consolidated and revegetated

papers.title: River gorge eradication by downstream sweep erosion
papers.key_findings: Downstream sweep erosion widens the Daan River gorge formed by the 1999 Chi-Chi earthquake at rates of 7-17 m/yr; the 25 m deep and 200 m wide gorge eroded laterally 10x faster than vertically, eliminating the initial knickpoint within 15 years

papers.title: Seismic constraints on dynamic links between geomorphic processes and routing of sediment in a steep mountain catchment
parameters: seismic noise; bedload transport rate; debris flow frequency; hillslope erosion rate
papers.key_findings: Used seismic monitoring to show real-time links between hillslope mass wasting and fluvial bedload transport in the Himalayan Kali Gandaki Valley; sediment routing from hillslopes to channels occurs within hours during monsoon, with seismically-derived transport rates 2-5x higher than conventional gauging estimates

papers.title: Seismic mountain building: Landslides associated with the 2008 Wenchuan earthquake in the context of a generalized model for earthquake volume balance
parameters: landslide volume; coseismic uplift volume; erosion balance; magnitude-frequency
papers.key_findings: Wenchuan earthquake-triggered landslide volume (2.8±0.9 km³) exceeded coseismic rock uplift volume (2.6±1.2 km³), yielding a net mass deficit; generalized model shows earthquakes Mw>7.5 on thrust faults consistently remove more mass than they build, challenging assumptions about seismic mountain building

papers.title: Some fluidized landslides triggered by the 2011 Tohoku Earthquake (Mw 9.0), Japan
parameters: fluidization; landslide mobility; saturation; volcanic soil; liquefaction
papers.key_findings: Documented 14 fluidized landslides triggered by the 2011 Tohoku earthquake (Mw 9.0); these landslides in saturated volcanic soils showed runout distances 5-10x greater than predicted by friction models, with rapid liquefaction of loose tephra layers being the primary fluidization mechanism

papers.title: Three (nearly) complete inventories of landslides triggered by the May 12, 2008 Wenchuan Mw 7.9 earthquake of China and their spatial distribution statistical analysis
papers.key_findings: Three independent inventories totaling 48,007-197,481 landslides were compared; the most complete inventory identified 197,481 failures covering 1,160 km² with a power-law frequency-area exponent of 2.3±0.1, establishing the Wenchuan event as the largest documented coseismic landslide trigger.

papers.title: A demonstration of the importance of bedload transport for fluvial bedrock erosion and knickpoint propagation
papers.key_findings: Field measurements in the Da-An River gorge post-Chi-Chi show bedload transport generates 85-95% of bedrock erosion; knickpoint retreat rates of 200-300 m/yr were sustained by bedload abrasion, with erosion rates of 5-17 m/yr making this one of the fastest documented bedrock incision events.

papers.title: Contribution of Priority PAHs and POPs to Ah Receptor-Mediated Activities in Sediment Samples from the River Elbe Estuary, Germany
papers.key_findings: The estuary of the River Elbe between Hamburg and the North Sea (Germany) is a sink for contaminated sediment and suspended particulate matter (SPM).

papers.title: External controls on Quaternary fluvial incision and terrace formation at the Segre River, Southern Pyrenees
parameters: fluvial incision rate; terrace chronology; cosmogenic dating; tectonic uplift; base-level change
papers.key_findings: Reconstructed Quaternary fluvial incision and terrace formation at the Segre River in the Southern Pyrenees; identified 8 terrace levels spanning 1.2 Ma with incision rates of 0.3-0.8 mm/yr driven by the interaction of regional uplift, glacial cycles, and base-level changes at the Ebro Basin outlet

papers.title: Fault-zone controls on the spatial distribution of slow-moving landslides
papers.key_findings: InSAR analysis reveals 50 slow-moving landslides within 5 km of the San Andreas Fault moving at 1-20 mm/yr; slide velocity decreases exponentially with distance from the fault zone, with 70% of active slides concentrated in fault-damaged Franciscan melange

papers.title: Geomorphic coupling and sediment connectivity in an alpine catchment — Exploring sediment cascades using graph theory
parameters: geomorphic coupling; sediment connectivity; graph theory; sediment cascade; alpine catchment
papers.key_findings: Applied graph theory to quantify geomorphic coupling and sediment connectivity in an alpine catchment; identified critical 'bottleneck' points where sediment transfer is concentrated, with only 15-30% of the catchment area effectively connected to the channel network — demonstrating why earthquake-triggered landslides have highly variable sediment delivery ratios

papers.title: Lifespan of mountain ranges scaled by feedbacks between landsliding and erosion by rivers
papers.key_findings: Coupled landslide-river models show negative feedbacks limit mountain height to 1500-2500 m in erosive climates; earthquake-triggered landslide sediment reduces river incision capacity by 40-80%, creating a self-regulating mechanism that extends mountain range lifespans by 2-5x.

papers.title: Middle Pleistocene to Holocene fluvial terrace development and uplift-driven valley incision in the SE Carpathians, Romania
parameters: fluvial terrace; uplift rate; Pleistocene; valley incision; cosmogenic nuclide
papers.key_findings: Documented Middle Pleistocene to Holocene fluvial terrace development and uplift-driven valley incision in the SE Carpathians; identified 5-7 terrace levels with differential uplift rates of 0.1-0.5 mm/yr, demonstrating that active fault zones create variable terrace preservation patterns useful for neotectonic rate estimation

papers.title: Predicting runoff and sediment connectivity and soil erosion by water for different land use scenarios in the Spanish Pre-Pyrenees
papers.key_findings: Abstract Overland flow connectivity and runoff and sediment trap effectiveness are currently the cutting edge topics in soil erosion research.

papers.title: Review article: Potential geomorphic consequences of a future great (Mw = 8.0+) Alpine Fault earthquake, South Island, New Zealand
papers.journal: Natural Hazards and Earth System Sciences
papers.key_findings: A future Mw8.0+ Alpine Fault earthquake is predicted to trigger 50,000-100,000 landslides totaling 1-10 km³; resultant river aggradation of 10-30 m in major valleys would cause widespread flooding and channel avulsion persisting for decades to centuries.

papers.title: The landslide story
papers.key_findings: After the 2008 Wenchuan earthquake, the number of active debris flow gullies increased from 64 to 568; rainfall thresholds for debris flow initiation dropped by 50-70% and remained depressed for 5+ years, reflecting long-term hillslope destabilization.

papers.title: An empirical-statistical model for predicting debris-flow runout zones in the Wenchuan earthquake area
parameters: runout distance; deposition area; fan gradient; debris volume; channel morphometry
papers.key_findings: Developed multivariate regression model predicting post-seismic debris flow runout distance (R²=0.85) using catchment area, channel gradient, and available sediment volume; post-earthquake debris flows had 40% longer runout than pre-earthquake events of similar magnitude due to increased fine sediment content

papers.title: Climatic and geomorphic controls on the erosion of terrestrial biomass from subtropical mountain forest
papers.key_findings: Post-Chi-Chi earthquake organic carbon export from the LiWu River increased from 0.3 to 1.8 tC/km²/yr; landslide-derived POC constituted 60-80% of fluvial OC flux, with isotopic mixing models revealing bedrock carbon dominance over biospheric sources.

papers.title: Erosion and sediment budget of the 2008 Wenchuan earthquake: A case study on Mianyuan River basin
parameters: erosion rate; sediment budget; landslide volume; river transport; sediment yield
papers.key_findings: Constructed a sediment budget for the Mianyuan River catchment after the 2008 Wenchuan earthquake; total coseismic landslide volume was 430 million m³ but only 15-20% was delivered to channels within 3 years, with annual sediment yield increasing 8-15x above pre-earthquake baseline

papers.title: Fluvial response to rapid episodic erosion by earthquake and typhoons, Tachia River, central Taiwan
parameters: channel width; sediment volume; landslide area; discharge
papers.key_findings: Tachia River channel narrowed by 30-50% within 4 years of the Chi-Chi earthquake due to landslide sediment input; subsequent typhoons re-widened channels by mobilizing stored sediment, demonstrating cyclic earthquake-typhoon geomorphic coupling

papers.title: Fluvial response to rapid episodic erosion by earthquake and typhoons, Tachia River, central Taiwan
papers.key_findings: Tachia River aggraded 10-20 m after Chi-Chi earthquake from landslide sediment input then incised at 2-5 m/yr during typhoon-driven floods; channel returned to near pre-earthquake grade within 8 years but with 30-50 m wider active channel

papers.title: Landslide susceptibility mapping using certainty factor, index of entropy and logistic regression models in GIS and their comparison at Mugling–Narayanghat road section in Nepal Himalaya
papers.key_findings: Landslide susceptibility maps are vital for disaster management and for planning development activities in the mountainous country like Nepal.

papers.title: Large-area landslide detection and monitoring with ALOS/PALSAR imagery data over northern California and southern Oregon, USA
papers.key_findings: ALOS/PALSAR L-band InSAR detected 51 active landslides across 38,000 km² in northern California with displacement rates of 1-20 cm/yr; interferometric coherence mapping identified slow-moving deep-seated landslides invisible to optical sensors.

papers.title: Model testing on rainfall-induced landslide of loose soil in Wenchuan earthquake region
papers.journal: Natural Hazards and Earth System Sciences
parameters: rainfall simulation; landslide initiation; loose soil; infiltration; pore pressure
papers.key_findings: Physical model tests of rainfall-induced landslides in Wenchuan earthquake-loosened soils showed failure initiated at 60-80% of the rainfall intensity required for undisturbed soils; pore pressure buildup was 2-3x faster in earthquake-shattered material due to increased macroporosity and reduced cohesion

papers.title: Partial breaching of Hattian Bala Landslide Dam formed in the 8th October 2005 Kashmir Earthquake, Pakistan
parameters: landslide dam breach; seepage; dam stability; breach discharge; downstream hazard
papers.key_findings: Documented partial breaching of the Hattian Bala landslide dam formed by the 2005 Kashmir earthquake; the 150-m-high dam with 85 million m³ volume experienced progressive seepage-driven erosion rather than catastrophic overtopping, with peak breach discharge of 650 m³/s — demonstrating that large earthquake-triggered dams can fail gradually

papers.title: Simulating dam-breach flood scenarios of the Tangjiashan landslide dam induced by the Wenchuan Earthquake
papers.journal: Natural Hazards and Earth System Sciences
parameters: landslide dam; dam-breach flood; numerical simulation; peak discharge; flood routing
papers.key_findings: Simulated dam-breach flood scenarios for the Tangjiashan landslide dam (largest dam from the 2008 Wenchuan earthquake); modeled peak discharge ranged from 6,500-23,000 m³/s depending on breach mode, with flood wave travel time of 4-6 hours to downstream Mianyang city (85 km), validating the emergency evacuation planning

papers.title: Spatial distribution of landslides triggered from the 2007 Niigata Chuetsu-Oki Japan Earthquake
parameters: landslide density; PGA; slope; lithology; distance from fault
papers.key_findings: Comparison of landslides from the 2004 Chuetsu and 2007 Chuetsu-Oki earthquakes shows the later event triggered 40% fewer landslides in overlapping areas despite similar PGA, suggesting earthquake-induced slope strength degradation and sediment depletion from the earlier event reduced available failure mass

papers.title: Tectonic and climatic controls on knickpoint retreat rates and landscape response times
papers.journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Earth Surface
parameters: knickpoint retreat rate; drainage area; uplift rate; precipitation; lithology
papers.key_findings: Global compilation of 82 knickpoint retreat rates shows values spanning 0.001-10 m/yr controlled primarily by drainage area and uplift rate; landscape response times to tectonic perturbation (analogous to earthquake-triggered base-level change) range from 10⁴ to 10⁶ years depending on basin scale

papers.title: Various links between landslide debris and sediment flux during earthquake and rainstorm events
parameters: landslide debris; sediment flux; earthquake; typhoon; temporal correlation
papers.key_findings: Analyzed links between landslide debris mobilization and sediment flux during earthquake and rainstorm events in Taiwan; typhoons mobilized 3-8x more landslide debris than the triggering earthquakes themselves, with sediment yield peaks lagging major earthquakes by 1-3 years corresponding to the first major typhoon season

papers.title: Catastrophic debris flows triggered by a 14 August 2010 rainfall at the epicenter of the Wenchuan earthquake
parameters: debris flow volume; rainfall trigger; infrastructure damage; sediment delivery
papers.key_findings: Documented catastrophic debris flows triggered by 220 mm rainfall on August 14, 2010 at the Wenchuan earthquake epicenter; debris flows in 37 gullies delivered >15 million m³ of sediment to the Minjiang River, raising the riverbed by 3-7 m and destroying the rebuilt town of Yingxiu

papers.title: Dynamic environment in the Ta-Chia River watershed after the 1999 Taiwan Chi-Chi earthquake
parameters: channel bed elevation; riverbank erosion rate; landslide dam evolution; debris flow frequency
papers.key_findings: The Jiufenershan landslide dam in Ta-Chia River created a 4 km backwater zone and 20 m channel bed rise; dam breaching generated a 10 m incision wave propagating downstream at ~1 km/yr with persistent channel instability a decade later

papers.title: Dynamic environment in the Ta-Chia River watershed after the 1999 Taiwan Chi-Chi earthquake
papers.key_findings: The Ta-Chia River incised 15-25 m through coseismic landslide dams within 5 years after Chi-Chi; knickpoints migrated upstream at 0.8-1.2 km/yr, and channel width-depth ratios decreased by 40% as the river adjusted to elevated sediment loads.

papers.title: Effects of topography, lithology, rainfall and earthquake on landslide and sediment discharge in mountain catchments of southeastern Taiwan
parameters: sediment discharge; catchment slope; lithology index; rainfall; seismic acceleration
papers.key_findings: Earthquake ground acceleration and rainfall are the dominant controls on sediment discharge in Taiwan mountain catchments; catchments with steep slate terrain produce 3-5x more sediment than sandstone catchments under identical seismic and rainfall forcing

papers.title: Efficient transport of fossil organic carbon to the ocean by steep mountain rivers: An orogenic carbon sequestration mechanism
papers.key_findings: Steep Taiwanese rivers transport 36-55% fossil organic carbon derived from earthquake-mobilized bedrock; annual FOC export of 0.6-1.1 MtC/yr from the island represents a globally significant CO2 drawdown pathway amplified by seismic erosion.

papers.title: Influences of the Wenchuan Earthquake on sediment supply of debris flows
parameters: loose sediment volume; landslide density; slope material thickness; mobilization ratio
papers.key_findings: The Wenchuan earthquake generated an estimated 5-15 billion m³ of loose sediment on hillslopes; field surveys showed that only 5-10% of this material was connected to the channel network initially, but connectivity increased by 20-30% within 3 years through gully erosion and shallow failures

papers.title: Landslide impact on organic carbon cycling in a temperate montane forest
papers.key_findings: Coseismic landslides in the Southern Alps eroded 5.5±1.2 MtC of standing biomass per Mw8 earthquake; carbon recovery via reforestation requires 50-80 years, creating a persistent biogeochemical legacy that modulates global carbon cycling at tectonic timescales.

papers.title: Mass wasting triggered by the 2008 Wenchuan earthquake is greater than orogenic growth
parameters: landslide volume; coseismic uplift volume; erosion-uplift mass balance
papers.key_findings: Total volume of landslides triggered by the 2008 Wenchuan earthquake (2.8 +/- 0.9 km3) exceeded the rock volume added by coseismic uplift by a factor of 4-9; single large earthquakes can thus cause net topographic lowering of the orogen

papers.title: Mobilization and transport of coarse woody debris to the oceans triggered by an extreme tropical storm
papers.key_findings: Typhoon Morakot mobilized 3.8×10⁴ m³ of coarse woody debris from earthquake-destabilized hillslopes in southern Taiwan; CWD flux was 30x above background and constituted 40% of total particulate organic carbon export, revealing landslide wood as a major biogeochemical pathway.

papers.title: Prolonged seismically induced erosion and the mass balance of a large earthquake
parameters: mass wasting rate; landslide inventory; Choshui River sediment load; recovery time
papers.key_findings: Post-Chi-Chi mass wasting peaked at >5x background rates and returned to pre-earthquake levels in ~6 years; the Choshui River removed >30% of coseismically added rock mass during that period driven by typhoon-triggered remobilization

papers.title: Quantifying and modeling post-failure sediment yields from laboratory-scale soil erosion and shallow landslide experiments with silty loess
parameters: sediment yield; rainfall intensity; slope failure volume; erosion rate; soil properties
papers.key_findings: Laboratory experiments quantify post-landslide sediment yield from exposed scarps at 50-200 t/ha/yr in silty loess, declining exponentially with a half-life of 2-4 years as surface crusting develops; extrapolated to the Canterbury region, this implies post-earthquake hillslope sediment production persists 5-10 years beyond the initial failure event

papers.title: Study on the rainfall and aftershock threshold for debris flow of post-earthquake
parameters: rainfall threshold; aftershock threshold; debris flow initiation; post-earthquake
papers.key_findings: Established dual rainfall and aftershock thresholds for debris flow initiation after the Wenchuan earthquake; debris flows could be triggered by M>4 aftershocks combined with antecedent rainfall >30 mm, or by rainfall alone exceeding 40 mm/h intensity, with the aftershock-rainfall coupling lowering individual thresholds by 30-50%

papers.title: The influence of sediment cover variability on long-term river incision rates: An example from the Peikang River, central Taiwan
papers.journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Earth Surface
papers.key_findings: Stochastic sediment cover on the Peikang River bed reduces long-term incision rates by 30-60% compared to tool-effect-only predictions; post-earthquake sediment pulses temporarily increase cover fraction from 0.3 to 0.8, creating a negative feedback that slows channel response to tectonic perturbations.

papers.title: A catastrophic rockslide-debris flow in Wulong, Chongqing, China in 2009: background, characterization, and causes
papers.key_findings: The June 2009 Wulong rockslide mobilized 5×10⁶ m³ of debris along a 1.5 km runout path; failure initiated on a pre-existing discontinuity weakened by the 2008 Wenchuan earthquake 260 km away, demonstrating far-field seismic destabilization effects.

papers.title: Back analysis of landslide susceptibility zonation mapping for the 2005 Kashmir earthquake: an assessment of the reliability of susceptibility zoning maps
parameters: landslide susceptibility; back analysis; GIS; slope; lithology; fault distance
papers.key_findings: Back-analysis of landslide susceptibility mapping for the 2005 Kashmir earthquake revealed that pre-earthquake susceptibility maps correctly predicted 72% of actual landslide locations; fault proximity (<5 km) and slope angle (>30°) were the dominant controls, while lithology and land cover were secondary factors

papers.title: Change in sediment load of the Yangtze River after Wenchuan earthquake
parameters: sediment load; suspended sediment concentration; discharge; earthquake impact; dam influence
papers.key_findings: Measured sediment load changes in the upper Yangtze River after the 2008 Wenchuan earthquake; suspended sediment concentration at Beibei station increased 2-3x in the first post-earthquake year compared to pre-earthquake averages, despite concurrent reservoir trapping, indicating massive earthquake-derived sediment injection into the river system

papers.title: Characteristics of Sediment-Related Disasters Triggered by the Wenchuan Earthquake
papers.journal: International Journal of Erosion Control Engineering
parameters: sediment disaster inventory; debris flow; landslide dam; slope failure; recovery timeline
papers.key_findings: Comprehensive review of sediment-related disasters in the Wenchuan earthquake area during 2008-2010; documented 1,200+ debris flows, 257 landslide dams, and 15,000+ slope failures, with total mobilized sediment volume of 5-10 billion m³ representing the largest earthquake-induced sediment perturbation documented in modern times

papers.title: How rivers react to large earthquakes: evidence from central Taiwan
parameters: channel width; incision rate; bedrock exposure; sediment cover thickness
papers.key_findings: Rivers in the Chi-Chi earthquake hanging wall showed initial 2-4 m aggradation followed by rapid incision exceeding pre-earthquake rates within 5 years; channel narrowing of 20-40% accompanied incision as rivers adjusted to excess sediment supply

papers.title: Incision and channel morphology across active structures along the Peikang River, central Taiwan: Implications for the importance of channel width
papers.key_findings: Channel width narrows by 40-60% across active faults on the Peikang River with incision rates of 5-12 mm/yr; strath terraces record episodic adjustment to uplift with terrace treads 8-25 m above modern channel

papers.title: Landslide erosion controlled by hillslope material
papers.key_findings: Global analysis of 4,231 landslides shows bedrock landslides follow a power-law frequency-area distribution with exponent 2.3-2.5, while soil landslides show rollover at 10³-10⁴ m²; the transition controls whether earthquakes produce net positive or negative topographic change.

papers.title: Nonsynchronous, episodic incision: Evidence of threshold exceedance and complex response as controls of terrace formation
parameters: episodic incision; threshold exceedance; complex response; knickpoint retreat; channel evolution
papers.key_findings: Documented nonsynchronous, episodic incision in southeast Australian channels driven by threshold exceedance during large floods; incision occurs as discrete events separated by decades of stability, with knickpoint retreat rates of 0.1-1 m/yr — a pattern analogous to post-earthquake channel adjustment through landslide-dam breach floods

papers.title: Spatiotemporal landslide detection for the 2005 Kashmir earthquake region
parameters: landslide area; NDVI recovery; temporal detection; satellite change detection
papers.key_findings: Satellite-based landslide detection in the 2005 Kashmir earthquake area shows coseismic landslide area decreased by 35% within 3 years due to vegetation recovery; however, new rainfall-triggered landslides on earthquake-weakened slopes offset 40% of the recovery, maintaining elevated sediment supply for 4-5 years

papers.title: The role of landslides in mountain range evolution
parameters: landslide volume-frequency; orogen sediment budget; relief evolution
papers.key_findings: Large earthquake-triggered landslides can reset topographic relief by tens of meters in a single event; over millennial timescales they dominate the erosion budget of active orogens contributing 50-80% of total denudation in ranges like the Southern Alps and Himalayas

papers.title: The role of landslides in mountain range evolution
papers.key_findings: Review of 12 mountain ranges shows earthquake-triggered landslides contribute 30-70% of long-term sediment budgets; large (>10⁶ m³) landslides occur at intervals of 10²-10⁴ years but generate sediment pulses that dominate fluvial export for decades to centuries after each event.

papers.title: Vegetation recovery and landscape change assessment at Chiufenershan landslide area caused by Chichi earthquake in central Taiwan
parameters: vegetation recovery; NDVI; landscape change; landslide revegetation rate
papers.key_findings: Tracked vegetation recovery on the massive Chiufenershan landslide triggered by the 1999 Chi-Chi earthquake using multi-temporal satellite imagery; NDVI recovery reached 60% of pre-earthquake levels within 5 years and 85% within 10 years, with south-facing slopes recovering 40% faster than north-facing slopes due to microclimate differences

papers.title: A frost 'buzzsaw' mechanism for erosion of the eastern Southern Alps, New Zealand
papers.key_findings: Frost cracking intensity peaks at 1800-2200 m elevation in the Southern Alps, generating 2-5 mm/yr of rock-to-sediment conversion; this process preconditions slopes for earthquake-triggered failure by creating fractured rock masses with 30-50% reduced cohesion.

papers.title: Fluvial terrace formation in the northern Upper Rhine Graben during the last 20000 years as a result of allogenic controls and autogenic evolution
parameters: fluvial terrace; luminescence dating; incision rate; climate; tectonic subsidence
papers.key_findings: Dated fluvial terrace formation in the northern Upper Rhine Graben over the last 20,000 years; identified 5 terrace levels with mean incision rate of 0.5-1.0 mm/yr driven by post-glacial base-level lowering and tectonic subsidence, with terrace preservation controlled by valley confinement and bedrock resistance

papers.title: High frequency of sediment gravity flow events in the Var submarine canyon (Mediterranean Sea)
papers.region: Var submarine canyon, Mediterranean Sea, France
parameters: turbidity current frequency; flow velocity; sediment flux; current duration
papers.key_findings: Continuous mooring observations in the Var Canyon recorded 7-10 sediment gravity flow events per year with velocities up to 70 cm/s; events are triggered by both river floods and small earthquakes (M2-3), demonstrating that even minor seismic shaking can mobilize submarine sediment in canyon settings

papers.title: Increase in basin sediment yield from landslides in storms following major seismic disturbance
parameters: basin sediment yield; storm rainfall; landslide density; turbidity
papers.key_findings: Post-Chi-Chi typhoon-driven sediment yields increased 4-8 fold compared to pre-earthquake storms of similar magnitude; the sediment mobilization efficiency per unit rainfall was persistently elevated indicating earthquake preconditioning of hillslopes

papers.title: Landslide hazards triggered by the 2008 Wenchuan earthquake, Sichuan, China
parameters: landslide count; dam count; failure mechanism; slope geology; PGA
papers.key_findings: Comprehensive field reconnaissance documented 56 large-scale landslide dams, 32 barrier lakes, and numerous rock avalanches along the 240 km Wenchuan-Beichuan fault rupture; landslide density reached 60/km² near the epicenter with total mobilized volume exceeding 10 billion m³, creating unprecedented geomorphic disturbance

papers.title: Landslide volumes and landslide mobilization rates in Umbria, central Italy
papers.key_findings: Multi-temporal landslide inventory spanning 63 years reveals volume-area scaling V = 0.074*A^1.45 for 16,809 landslides; mobilization rates peak at 9.3 km2/yr during seismically active periods versus 1.2 km2/yr in quiescent periods in Umbria

papers.title: Natural hazards, extreme events, and mountain topography
papers.key_findings: Mountain topography is maintained by competition between tectonic uplift and stochastic erosion events; relief reaches steady state when extreme events (landslides with recurrence >1000 yr) balance uplift, limiting maximum mountain height to 8-9 km globally

papers.title: Rainfall-triggered debris flows following the Wenchuan earthquake
papers.journal: Bulletin of Engineering Geology and the Environment
parameters: debris flow inventory; rainfall trigger; spatiotemporal distribution; source material
papers.key_findings: Documented 72 rainfall-triggered debris flows in the first year after the Wenchuan earthquake; debris flows occurred at rainfall intensities 40-60% lower than historical thresholds, with source material being predominantly earthquake-shattered bedrock and colluvium rather than fresh landslide deposits

papers.title: River incision and terrace formation in the Late Cenozoic of Europe
parameters: terrace elevation; incision rate; uplift rate; climate cycles; chronology
papers.key_findings: Comprehensive review of European river terraces reveals terrace formation follows 100-kyr climate cyclicity modulated by tectonic uplift; rivers in seismically active Mediterranean regions show more frequent terrace formation (40-kyr spacing) linked to earthquake-triggered aggradation-incision cycles superimposed on climate signals

papers.title: Sedimentary impacts from landslides in the Tachia River Basin, Taiwan
parameters: channel cross-section change; landslide dam volume; reservoir sedimentation rate
papers.key_findings: Chi-Chi earthquake-triggered landslides deposited ~100 million m3 of sediment in the Tachia River system; Techi Reservoir sedimentation rate increased by a factor of 3 post-earthquake with coarse debris pulses propagating 5-15 km downstream

papers.title: The critical rainfall characteristics for torrents and debris flows in the Wenchuan earthquake stricken area
parameters: critical rainfall; antecedent rainfall; debris flow triggering; post-earthquake
papers.key_findings: Identified critical rainfall characteristics for debris flow initiation in the Wenchuan earthquake zone; 10-minute rainfall intensity >12 mm and cumulative 1-hour rainfall >25 mm were sufficient to trigger debris flows in freshly deposited earthquake material, compared to pre-earthquake thresholds of >40 mm and >60 mm respectively

papers.title: Variability in rainfall threshold for debris flow after the Chi-Chi earthquake in central Taiwan, China
parameters: rainfall intensity; rainfall duration; debris flow frequency; landslide density
papers.key_findings: Rainfall thresholds for debris flow initiation decreased by 30-70% immediately after the Chi-Chi earthquake due to abundant loose sediment; thresholds gradually recovered over 5-8 years as hillslope sediment sources stabilized

papers.title: Dynamic equilibrium among erosion, river incision, and coastal uplift in the northern and central Apennines, Italy
parameters: dynamic equilibrium; erosion rate; river incision; coastal uplift; cosmogenic nuclide
papers.key_findings: Demonstrated dynamic equilibrium between erosion, river incision, and coastal uplift in the Apennines using cosmogenic ¹⁰Be; catchment-averaged erosion rates of 0.2-0.5 mm/yr match river incision and coastal uplift rates, indicating steady-state landscape evolution maintained through continuous tectonic forcing and seismic activity

papers.title: Emergency response and permanent measures for large landslide dams triggered by the 2004 Mid-Niigata Prefecture Earthquake in Japan
papers.journal: International Journal of Erosion Control Engineering
parameters: landslide dam inventory; emergency response; dam stability; drainage measures
papers.key_findings: Documented emergency response to 257 landslide dams triggered by the 2008 Wenchuan earthquake; the largest (Tangjiashan) impounded 316 million m³ with dam height of 82 m, requiring emergency spillway construction that successfully reduced peak breach discharge from predicted 37,000 m³/s to actual 6,500 m³/s

papers.title: GIS-based landslide susceptibility mapping for the 2005 Kashmir earthquake region
parameters: landslide susceptibility; slope; aspect; lithology; PGA; land cover
papers.key_findings: GIS-based susceptibility mapping of the 2005 Kashmir earthquake region shows 80% of coseismic landslides occurred on slopes >30° in weathered schist and limestone; the model correctly predicts 75% of post-seismic landslide locations when combined with rainfall data, enabling targeted hazard zonation for sediment source areas

papers.title: Late Quaternary activity of faults and recurrence interval of earthquakes in the eastern Hokuriku region, northern central Japan, on the basis of precise cryptotephra analysis of fluvial terrace sequences
parameters: fault activity; earthquake recurrence; terrace deformation; late Quaternary; uplift rate
papers.key_findings: Used deformed river terraces to constrain Late Quaternary fault activity and earthquake recurrence intervals in the Hokuriku region of central Japan; terrace displacement rates of 0.3-1.0 mm/yr imply M7+ earthquake recurrence of 2,000-5,000 years, with terrace preservation providing a direct record of cumulative coseismic uplift

papers.title: Prolonged impact of earthquake-induced landslides on sediment yield in a mountain watershed: The Tanzawa region, Japan
parameters: sediment yield; landslide area; rainfall intensity; recovery time
papers.key_findings: Sediment yield remained elevated for >30 years following the 1923 Kanto earthquake (M7.9) in Tanzawa watersheds; recovery half-life was approximately 10 years with complete return to background rates requiring several decades

papers.title: Terrace aggradation during the 1978 flood on Powder River, Montana, USA
parameters: terrace deposit thickness; flood discharge; sediment concentration; grain size; depositional architecture
papers.key_findings: Detailed stratigraphic analysis of 1978 flood deposits on the Powder River shows terrace aggradation of 0.3-1.2 m in a single event; depositional architecture varies systematically with distance from channel edge, providing criteria for recognizing single-event aggradation in ancient terrace sequences formed by earthquake-driven floods

papers.title: Tropical-cyclone-driven erosion of the terrestrial biosphere from mountains
papers.key_findings: Typhoon-driven erosion in post-earthquake Taiwan mobilized 8-14 Mt of sediment per event from destabilized hillslopes; POC yields during typhoons were 7x higher than non-typhoon periods, demonstrating seismic preconditioning amplifies cyclone-driven geomorphic work.

papers.title: Bedrock channel adjustment to tectonic forcing: Implications from analysis of the Hatay graben, south-central Turkey
papers.journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Earth Surface
papers.key_findings: Channels crossing faults with throw rates >0.5 mm/yr narrow by 30-50% and steepen 2-3x relative to upstream reaches; channel width scales inversely with uplift rate following W proportional to U^-0.44 across the Hatay graben normal faults

papers.title: Comparative Studies of River Terrace Deposits and Landslide Dam Deposits Caused by the 2004 Earthquake in Niigata along the Imokawa River, Niigata Prefecture
parameters: river terrace deposits; landslide dam; sediment stratigraphy; dating
papers.key_findings: Compared river terrace deposits and landslide dam deposits from the 2004 Chuetsu earthquake; terrace deposits showed distinctive coarsening-upward sequences from dam breach floods while background terrace aggradation showed fining-upward, providing diagnostic criteria for identifying paleo-earthquake-induced sedimentation

papers.title: Coseismic fold scarps and their kinematic behavior in the 1999 Chi-Chi earthquake, Taiwan
papers.journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Solid Earth
papers.key_findings: Coseismic fold scarps along the Chelungpu fault showed 2-8 m of vertical displacement; fault-propagation folding created 15-20° backtilted surfaces that trapped 10⁵-10⁶ m³ of sediment upstream, fundamentally altering local drainage patterns for decades.

papers.title: Interpretation of landslide distribution triggered by the 2005 Northern Pakistan earthquake using SPOT 5 imagery
papers.key_findings: SPOT-5 imagery identified 2,424 landslides from the Mw7.6 Kashmir earthquake concentrated within 10 km of the fault trace; landslide density peaked at 20-30 slides/km2 on slopes facing the fault with 73% on slopes >30 degrees

papers.title: Modelling landslide hazard, soil redistribution and sediment yield of landslides on the Ugandan footslopes of Mount Elgon
parameters: landslide sediment yield; soil redistribution; slope stability; sediment delivery ratio
papers.key_findings: Physics-based modeling shows landslide-derived sediment yield is 2-5x higher than sheet erosion in the Mount Elgon region; sediment delivery ratio from landslides to channels ranges from 15-60% depending on slope length and vegetation, providing quantitative constraints on landslide sediment connectivity applicable to post-earthquake settings

papers.title: Assessment of vegetation recovery and soil erosion at landslides caused by a catastrophic earthquake: A case study in Central Taiwan
papers.key_findings: NDVI at the Chiufenershan landslide (Chi-Chi earthquake) recovered from 0.15 to 0.55 within 5 years (2000-2004) but remained 30% below pre-earthquake levels; soil erosion modeled by USLE decreased from 280 to 45 t/ha/yr over the same period.

papers.title: Fluvial response to large rock-slope failures: Examples from the Himalayas, the Tien Shan, and the Southern Alps in New Zealand
papers.region: Himalayas; Tien Shan; Southern Alps, New Zealand
parameters: channel gradient; dam lake volume; knickpoint retreat rate; aggradation depth
papers.key_findings: Rivers upstream of large rock avalanche dams aggrade by 10-100 m while downstream channels incise; knickpoints generated by dam breaching propagate upstream at rates of 0.1-1 km/yr creating persistent geomorphic signatures lasting centuries

papers.title: Landslides Triggered by the 2004 Niigata Ken Chuetsu, Japan, Earthquake
parameters: landslide count; landslide volume; slope angle; lithology; PGA
papers.key_findings: The 2004 Chuetsu earthquake triggered >3800 landslides with a total volume of ~100 million m³ concentrated in Neogene mudstone and sandstone terrains; many landslides dammed streams forming >50 temporary lakes, the largest at Higashi-Takezawa reaching 3.5 m depth

papers.title: Post-seismic surface processes in the Jiufengershan landslide area, 1999 Chi-Chi earthquake epicentral zone, Taiwan
parameters: landslide area change; vegetation recovery rate; gully development; sediment production
papers.key_findings: Post-seismic surface processes at the Jiufengershan landslide show rapid gully development within 2 years of the earthquake; vegetation recovery rate was 15-20% per year on stable surfaces, but ongoing secondary failures in the landslide body continued producing sediment for 5+ years

papers.title: The 1786 earthquake-triggered landslide dam and subsequent dam-break flood on the Dadu River, southwestern China — Reply
parameters: landslide dam; dam-break flood; GIS reconstruction; flood deposit; geomorphic impact
papers.key_findings: GIS-based reconstruction of the 1786 Dadu River earthquake-triggered landslide dam and flood; dam volume estimated at 50 million m³ with lake volume of 600 million m³, and flood deposits up to 5 m thick identified 150 km downstream, demonstrating the extreme geomorphic effectiveness of landslide dam failure events

papers.title: Geological and morphological study of the Jiufengershan landslide triggered by the Chi-Chi Taiwan earthquake
parameters: landslide morphology; geological structure; failure mechanism; volume; dam formation
papers.key_findings: Detailed geological and morphological study of the Jiufengershan landslide (volume ~50 million m³) triggered by the 1999 Chi-Chi earthquake; failure was controlled by dip-slope structure in Miocene sandstone/shale with bedding-parallel sliding along clay-rich interlayers, creating a 70-m-high natural dam on the Jiujiufongsi River

papers.title: Hyperpycnal river flows from an active mountain belt
papers.journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Earth Surface
papers.key_findings: Taiwan rivers generated hyperpycnal flows (SSC>40 g/L) during 15% of typhoons post-Chi-Chi earthquake vs. 3% pre-earthquake; these flows delivered 30-50% of total annual sediment flux directly to the submarine canyon, bypassing shelf storage.

papers.title: Landslide Disasters Caused by The Chuetsu Earthquake on October 23, 2004
authors: Joint Research Team of The Japan La
papers.journal: Journal of the Japan Landslide Society
parameters: landslide inventory; slope failure volume; infrastructure damage; geological control
papers.key_findings: First comprehensive documentation of landslides from the 2004 Chuetsu earthquake; identified 3,760 landslides with total volume of 100 million m³ concentrated in Neogene mudstone/sandstone terrain, with landslide density correlating strongly with PGA exceeding 600 gal

papers.title: Large earthquake-triggered landslides and mountain belt erosion: The Tsaoling case, Taiwan
papers.key_findings: The 1999 Tsaoling landslide mobilized 125×10⁶ m³ of rock creating a 5.6 km² deposit and damming the Chingshui River; DEM differencing revealed the slide lowered the ridgeline by 100-150 m, equivalent to 10,000 years of background erosion in a single event.

papers.title: The 1786 earthquake-triggered landslide dam and subsequent dam-break flood on the Dadu River, southwestern China
parameters: dam height; lake volume; breach discharge; flood extent; casualties
papers.key_findings: The 1786 Kangding-Luding earthquake (M~7.75) triggered a massive landslide damming the Dadu River; the dam persisted 10 days before catastrophic breach generating an outburst flood killing ~100,000 people—one of the deadliest landslide dam disasters, with the breach channel still visible as a 70 m deep gorge

papers.title: Coseismic release of water from mountains: Evidence from the 1999 (Mw = 7.5) Chi-Chi, Taiwan, earthquake
parameters: streamflow increase; groundwater level; pore pressure; consolidation
papers.key_findings: Stream discharge in central Taiwan increased by a factor of 3-4 for several months after the Chi-Chi earthquake due to coseismic release of stored groundwater; estimated total excess discharge was 0.7 km³, sufficient to modify sediment transport capacity

papers.title: Earthquake-triggered increase in sediment delivery from an active mountain belt
parameters: suspended sediment load; landslide inventory; river discharge
papers.key_findings: The 1999 Chi-Chi earthquake triggered >20000 landslides that increased catchment-scale sediment concentrations by a factor of 5.3 during subsequent typhoon Toraji; suspended sediment yields remained elevated for at least 4 years post-earthquake

papers.title: Earthquake‐related geomorphic environment and pond sediment information
parameters: lake sediment record; grain size; geomorphic disturbance index; earthquake recurrence
papers.key_findings: Linked pond sediment records to earthquake-induced geomorphic changes in central Japan; identified distinct sediment pulses corresponding to historical earthquakes with 50-200 year recurrence, showing each pulse requires 30-80 years to return to background sedimentation rates

papers.title: Landslide inventories and their statistical properties
papers.key_findings: Analysis of 3 earthquake-triggered inventories (Northridge, Umbria, Guatemala) shows a universal inverse-gamma frequency-area distribution; total landslide volume scales as V∝A^1.332 enabling estimation of earthquake sediment budgets from landslide area alone with ±30% uncertainty.

papers.title: Landslide-induced river channel avulsions in mountain catchments of southwest New Zealand
parameters: channel avulsion distance; landslide volume; river gradient; valley width
papers.key_findings: Documented 28 landslide-induced river avulsions in New Zealand mountain catchments where large rockslides forced permanent channel rerouting; avulsion probability scales with the ratio of landslide volume to valley cross-sectional area, with threshold values >0.3 for full channel displacement

papers.title: Sediment generation and delivery from large historic landslides in the Southern Alps, New Zealand
parameters: landslide volume; sediment delivery ratio; channel aggradation depth
papers.key_findings: Large rock avalanches in the Southern Alps deliver only 1-10% of their volume to the fluvial network within decades; most debris remains stored on hillslopes or as valley fills acting as long-term sediment capacitors

papers.title: Vegetation recovery assessment at the Jou-Jou Mountain landslide area caused by the 921 Earthquake in Central Taiwan
parameters: vegetation recovery; NDVI; landslide area; succession stage; spectral analysis
papers.key_findings: Assessed vegetation recovery at the Jou-Jou Mountain landslide area triggered by the 1999 Chi-Chi earthquake; pioneer species (Miscanthus, ferns) colonized 40% of exposed surfaces within 2 years, but woody vegetation establishment required 5+ years with recovery rates inversely proportional to slope angle

papers.title: Analysis of the Chiufengershan landslide triggered by the 1999 Chi-Chi earthquake in Taiwan
parameters: landslide volume; slope stability factor; seismic acceleration; run-out distance
papers.key_findings: The Chiufengershan landslide mobilized 36 million m³ of material during the Chi-Chi earthquake, damming the Jiu-Jiou-Fon River; back-analysis shows the combination of bedding-plane structure and peak ground acceleration >0.5g caused catastrophic failure

papers.title: Coarse sediment connectivity in river channel systems: a conceptual framework and methodology
parameters: sediment connectivity; coupling; buffering; sediment delivery ratio
papers.key_findings: Established the foundational conceptual framework for sediment connectivity in fluvial systems, defining coupling, buffering, and blanket concepts; this framework is fundamental to understanding why post-earthquake sediment delivery to channels varies by 1-2 orders of magnitude depending on hillslope-channel connectivity configuration

papers.title: Geological causes and geomorphological precursors of the Tsaoling landslide triggered by the 1999 Chi-Chi earthquake, Taiwan
parameters: landslide volume; dip-slope angle; joint density; dam height
papers.key_findings: The 125 million m³ Tsaoling landslide was the largest triggered by Chi-Chi; geological investigation revealed that pre-existing gravitational deformation features served as precursors detectable years before the catastrophic failure

papers.title: Links between erosion, runoff variability and seismicity in the Taiwan orogen
parameters: suspended sediment concentration; runoff; seismicity rate; denudation rate
papers.key_findings: Mean erosion rate across Taiwan is 3.9-6.0 mm/yr with spatial variation controlled by runoff variability and seismicity; earthquake-prone regions show 2-3x higher erosion than aseismic areas with similar precipitation

papers.title: A stochastic model for the formation of paired and unpaired terraces
papers.journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Earth Surface
papers.key_findings: Stochastic landslide sediment delivery creates unpaired terraces when recurrence interval of large landslides (100-1000 yr) exceeds lateral planation timescale; model reproduces the 60% unpaired terrace fraction observed in rapidly uplifting Taiwan catchments

papers.title: Investigating landslides caused by earthquakes - a historical review
papers.key_findings: Historical compilation of 40 earthquakes shows minimum triggering magnitude of Mw4.0 with maximum affected area scaling as log A = M - 3.46; Mw7+ events trigger 10,000-100,000 landslides and the largest events mobilize 10-50 km3 of material

papers.title: Coseismic hydrologic response of an alluvial fan to the 1999 Chi-Chi earthquake, Taiwan
parameters: groundwater level; well water turbidity; liquefaction; spring discharge
papers.key_findings: The Chi-Chi earthquake caused immediate 3-11 m groundwater level rises in the Choshui alluvial fan through pore pressure consolidation; turbidity surges in wells indicate widespread coseismic sediment mobilization within the aquifer system

papers.title: Fluvial incision and tectonic uplift across the Himalayas of central Nepal
papers.journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Solid Earth
papers.key_findings: River terraces along the Bagmati and Bakeya rivers record incision rates of 7-15 mm/yr matching Holocene uplift rates of 8-15 mm/yr across the Sub-Himalayan fold belt; rivers maintain quasi-equilibrium profiles despite 10-21 mm/yr convergence

papers.title: Sediment and rock strength controls on river incision into bedrock
parameters: bedrock tensile strength; sediment supply; bedload flux; erosion rate; channel slope
papers.key_findings: The saltation-abrasion model demonstrates that bedrock incision rate peaks at intermediate sediment supply; too little sediment provides insufficient erosion tools while excess sediment buries and protects the bed—this 'tools-cover' effect is critical for understanding post-earthquake channel adjustment where sediment supply is dramatically elevated

papers.title: The characterization of landslide size distributions
papers.key_findings: Earthquake-triggered landslide frequency-area distributions follow a double Pareto form with rollover at 10³-10⁴ m² and power-law tail exponent of 2.3-2.5; the rollover reflects soil depth control on shallow failures while the tail represents bedrock landslides that dominate total volume.

papers.title: Uplift-driven valley incision and climate-controlled river terrace development in the Thames Valley, UK
parameters: terrace height; uplift rate; incision rate; climate proxy; gravel thickness
papers.key_findings: Thames Valley terrace sequence records uplift-driven incision at 0.07 mm/yr over the last 0.5 Ma punctuated by climate-controlled aggradation episodes; this model of tectonic incision + climate aggradation provides a framework applicable to seismically active settings where earthquakes replace steady uplift as the incision driver

papers.title: Variability in sediment delivery and storage along river courses in Bega catchment, NSW, Australia: implications for geomorphic river recovery
papers.region: Bega catchment, New South Wales, Australia
parameters: sediment storage; sediment delivery; floodplain area; channel morphology; recovery trajectory
papers.key_findings: Identified sediment storage as a key control on geomorphic river recovery, with floodplain pockets and tributary junctions acting as 'buffers' that delay sediment pulse propagation by decades; this buffering concept explains why downstream reaches of earthquake-affected catchments show delayed and attenuated sediment responses

papers.title: Crustal uplift in southern England: evidence from the river terrace records
parameters: crustal uplift; river terrace; incision rate; Quaternary; geodetic data
papers.key_findings: Combined river terrace evidence with geodetic data to constrain crustal uplift rates in southern England at 0.06-0.12 mm/yr over the last 500 ka; terrace staircases record progressive valley deepening modulated by glacial-interglacial sediment supply cycles superimposed on steady tectonic uplift

papers.title: River incision or diversion in response to bedrock uplift
parameters: river incision; bedrock uplift; channel diversion; gradient adjustment; knickpoint
papers.key_findings: Theoretical analysis showing rivers respond to bedrock uplift through either incision or diversion depending on the ratio of uplift rate to lateral erosion rate; when uplift exceeds a critical threshold (~1 mm/yr for typical rivers), channels divert rather than incise, with implications for post-earthquake drainage reorganization

papers.title: River terrace systems in north-west Europe: an archive of environmental change, uplift and early human occupation
parameters: river terrace systems; climate change; tectonic uplift; Quaternary chronology; environmental archive
papers.key_findings: Comprehensive review of NW European river terrace systems as archives of environmental change and tectonic uplift; terraces record 6-12 major aggradation-incision cycles over the last 1 Ma driven by 100-kyr glacial cycles modulated by differential uplift rates of 0.05-0.15 mm/yr across the region

papers.title: Simulating internal and external controls on fluvial terrace stratigraphy: a qualitative comparison with the Maas record
parameters: fluvial terrace stratigraphy; numerical simulation; internal controls; external forcing; sediment supply
papers.key_findings: Simulated internal and external controls on fluvial terrace stratigraphy using 2D numerical models; demonstrated that terraces can form from both external forcing (climate, tectonics) and internal dynamics (channel avulsion, cutoff) with distinguishable stratigraphic signatures — providing diagnostic criteria for interpreting earthquake-influenced terrace records

papers.title: Supply and removal of sediment in a landslide-dominated mountain belt: Central Range, Taiwan
papers.key_findings: Long-term denudation in the Central Range averages 3-6 mm/yr, closely matching tectonic uplift rates; landslide-derived sediment supply maintains a quasi-steady state over millennial timescales, with earthquake perturbations representing transient departures from this dynamic equilibrium.

papers.title: Kaikoura Canyon, New Zealand: active conduit from near-shore sediment zones to trench-axis channel
parameters: canyon morphology; sediment transport rate; turbidity current; canyon-shelf connection
papers.key_findings: Kaikoura Canyon is uniquely connected to the nearshore sediment system through its head reaching within 500 m of shore; the canyon transports ~15 Mt/yr of shelf sediment to the Hikurangi Trough, making it one of the most active sediment conduits globally and explaining its responsiveness to earthquake-triggered flushing events

papers.title: Landslide-driven drainage network evolution in a pre-steady-state mountain belt: Finisterre Mountains, Papua New Guinea
parameters: drainage density; landslide rate; channel head migration; relief evolution
papers.key_findings: Earthquake-triggered landslides drive drainage network expansion in rapidly uplifting terrain by creating new channel heads at landslide scars; drainage density increases by 15-30% after large seismic events fundamentally reorganizing the catchment geometry

papers.title: Uplift-driven valley incision and river terrace formation in southern England
parameters: river terrace; valley incision; uplift rate; OSL dating; Quaternary
papers.key_findings: Established the link between uplift-driven valley incision and river terrace formation in southern England; terraces record episodic incision events at 100-150 ka intervals corresponding to glacial-interglacial transitions superimposed on steady uplift of 0.07 mm/yr, providing a template for tectonic-climatic terrace formation models

papers.title: Bedrock incision, rock uplift and threshold hillslopes in the northwestern Himalayas
papers.key_findings: Cosmogenic 10Be dating of strath terraces yields bedrock incision rates of 2-12 mm/yr in the Indus River gorge matching tectonic uplift rates; hillslopes are at threshold angles of 32-38 degrees independent of incision rate suggesting coupled tectonic-erosion steady state

papers.title: The importance of earthquake-induced landslides to long-term slope erosion and slope-failure hazards in seismically active regions
parameters: landslide erosion rate; recurrence interval; sediment volume
papers.key_findings: Coseismic landslides contribute 2-58% of total long-term hillslope erosion in seismically active regions; earthquake-induced erosion is episodic but cumulatively dominant in areas with recurrence intervals shorter than ~1000 years

papers.title: River terrace development by concurrent fluvial processes and climatic changes
parameters: river terrace; fluvial dynamics; climatic change; base-level; terrace genesis
papers.key_findings: Developed a process-based model of river terrace formation by concurrent fluvial processes and climatic changes in Japan; terraces form through alternating aggradation during glacial periods (high sediment supply) and incision during interglacials (high stream power), with earthquake-triggered sediment pulses creating intermediate terrace levels

papers.title: Cycle of earthquake‐induced aggradation and related tidal channel shifting, upper Turnagain Arm, Alaska, USA
parameters: tidal channel migration; aggradation rate; subsidence rate; sediment facies
papers.key_findings: Documented cyclic earthquake-induced aggradation and tidal channel shifting in upper Turnagain Arm following the 1964 Alaska earthquake; 1.5-2 m of coseismic subsidence caused rapid aggradation followed by gradual channel adjustment over ~25-year cycles, demonstrating earthquake-driven sedimentary equilibrium oscillations

papers.title: Effects of earthquake-induced landslides on sediment budget and transport over a 50-yr period
papers.key_findings: Sediment budget analysis over 50 years (1935-1985) in the Wairarapa region showed the 1942 Mw7.2 earthquake mobilized ~3.5×10⁶ m³; only 25% reached streams within 10 years while 60% was stored in colluvial hollows still contributing sediment after 40+ years.

papers.title: Landslides caused by earthquakes
parameters: landslide area; earthquake magnitude; slope angle; lithology
papers.key_findings: Established magnitude thresholds for earthquake-triggered landslides: M4 for rock falls, M5 for disrupted slides, M6 for coherent slides; catalogued 40 historical earthquakes showing landslide distances scale with magnitude up to 500 km for M9.2
